Managing the pantry inventory is also very slow and unreliable. Selecting "Remove" does not work at some times. I will not be renewing m

how does it work? no idea. it wouldn't let me get past the "link to stores" section of starting an account with the app, expecting you to make separate accounts for each store, & the fact I NEVER wanted to link a store wasn't considered as an option. I thought this app was supposed to be helpful for tracking ingredients & coming up with recipes they can be used in, but nope. huge focus on shopping before you even get to actual content. this app is waste of phone storage.

I only spent about an hour trying to set it up, so I gave one more star than I would if I'd used it longer. Seems useful in theory, not as much in practice. Adding items was frustrating because the unit of measurement was unclear. "1 rice" isn't clear to me. Including a unit next to the quantity selector would help (lb, 8 oz bottle, 12 pack, etc). I'm sure I'm missing it, but I didn't see a way to connect to my King Soopers account. It kept resetting my edited expiration dates.

This app has changed for the worse. I still use it but currently trying to find an alternative. still saves recipes and helps plan meals. It use to be a 5 star app when you it would connect to stores and show you price difference and when you could export the shopping list to a grocery store app for easier checking out.

I just made some really good chicken with stuff I already had at home! it was super easy and I was able to set it to 1 serving and the recipe was exactly 1 serving it was perfect because my husband works nights and I eat dinner alone and I always make too much food. I also struggle with not knowing what to cook and feeling like I "have nothing to eat" so I love that I can set it to only show me recipes I can cook right now with what I have no trips to the store required. 5 stars. love this app

Fake price for a grocery item in a Google shopping search leads one to the app install. Before letting you do anything, the app runs you through a bunch of personal questions before dropping that the app itself costs money, and the grocery item isn't actually available at the price it was advertised at. Doesn't seem trustworthy.

Just started using it, and I really like it so far. I love that I can filter recipes by what I have in my pantry. A few things I'd like: let me shop at Meijer. Some way to track leftovers. When you say you cooked a recipe and it asks if you want to update your pantry, start with the ingredients from the recipe you cooked. Some way to better handle meal prep or food preservation, such as canning or freezer meals. Overall, a good app. Just could use a few more facets.

This app is fantastic! I really do love it, it's convenient and through. However, giving 3 stars because originally set my settings to be both vegan and low-carb (a very tricky combo) and was thrilled with the recipes. I accidentally tapped them off and now I have to pay to turn them back on! Now I must personally make sure every vegan recipe is low-carb, meaning I spend WAY more time looking through recipes than I'd like to.

This is a decent pantry manager/ meal planning app that is relatively easy to use. Which isn't saying much because if/when I find a better one I'll switch for the reasons I'm about to list. First, frequently recipes don't import correctly and I have to edit after import. Secondly, there is no web interface, only the app and while the app is good and handy. I much prefer meal planning from my computer so not having web access is a big disadvantage.

Really good inventory app! I love that I can link it to my Walmart shopping and it automatically adds everything to my pantry. The only downside is not being able to count items in percentages over ONE. So if you have a whole bag and another HALF bag of say, pizza rolls, you have to input TWO. But once you get to ONE, you can start subtracting used portions in 10% increments. I'd also like to do butcher cut meats like ground beef in POUNDS. But other than that minor issue, I'm loving this app.

Love the concept and most of the way it works. Would love it if you guys added more stores. My primary store is Aldi, but it is not available here. I've also been having issues with the "my recipes" tab. When trying to edit/remix recipes that I've imported, the edits don't save. Upon closing the app and reopening, it goes back to the way it was before.

Love it so far! Does everything for me, it's linked to my grocery shopping so it adds ingredients to my cart, I just check out. Then my pantry is automatically updated with my food purchases and the quantities are depleted as I cook. I know what I've got and I know what I need. It even keeps track of other household products like beauty products or paper products. Really awesome app!

This app is perfect, amazing, outstanding! I've been looking for an app for so long that would help me with everything I need when it comes to feeding my family. I don't usually write reviews (ever) but this app is by far the best combo of recipes, inventory, meal planning, shopping list, it has it all! I'm someone who struggles every day in life. This app makes me want to go though my house and scan everything! I could go on but go ahead and try for yourself. Sync your stores as well!

I found this app recommended by a social media user to help with budget planning and meal prep. Its definitely missing some features, or if it has them I have no idea how to make it work. For example: I'm cooking for one and then I eat the leftovers. I want to plan out which days I'm cooking and which ones I'm eating leftovers. I have not figured out how to add my leftovers to my lunch or dinner categories in the meal plan. Some of the missing ingredient suggestions are way off.

Could use some improvements! So I really like the idea of this app and it has some really great features but could use some improvements. I have a lot of recipes that I would love to add but I do not want to spend the time typing in the intirer recipes. It would be nice if you could just snap a picture of the recipes and it is added to your list of recipes. Also for people on a budget it would be great to add store like Aldi, LidlL, Sam's club, Costco, save a lot, and trader Joe's.

This app is amazing. I just got it set up. My goal is to stop waisting food and spending so much money on groceries. A couple of things I've noticed so far that I wish were better: I wish that when creating a recipe, we can credit a website or business name of who came up with the recipe. The other thing is that we are big on tracking our macros. It would be helpful if the nutrition facts broke down what ingredient is creating the macro number. Other than that, so far, I think it's great.

Great app! My biggest problem with cooking at home is that if my pantry isn't organized, I can't see what it is I have to cook with. This helps me understand what's in my pantry so I can get ideas of what to cook and I don't get overwhelmed. With everything in one place, I can see what I've spent in the last 30 days on groceries, search for recipes, plan my shopping list, and track expiration dates. I've saved so much money and gained confidence that I really can cook--even when I'm busy.

I am disabled and have caregivers to manage my house. App is no longer connecting to my chosen grocery store's system (Fry's Food Store). it is no longer able to retrieve the list of groceries I've purchased. I haven't been able to import for the last two months. I generally have too many items to list and photo manually. This app is no longer working for me. If it would reconnect and import my purchases, I would restore my previous rating.

This app lured me in after I Google search for an item and cooklist showed it in stock for a lot less than any other store. But I had to install the app to get to the item. And when installed the app there was no item or way to get to the item. It said Target had it but when I searched target they didn't show the item at all!!! Sad bait and switch tactic!!!!

Just downloaded, I am having issue with adding multiples of a item. Scanned it asks if you want to add it even though it's in your pantry. Does not add have to cancel and just toggle the up down quantity. Has extensive library of items. This makes it easy to add things like ketchup, salt ect. I have not used the pantry/recipe function yet. If allowed I'll update this review in a few days. It's going to take a while for me to add all we have. My use is to inventory, we stock for about 3 mths out.

Edit: Customer support informed me that this feature no longer exists at all on any version. Original: This seems like a useful tool for meal planning and grocery lists, but I was disappointed to see that the Android version of the app doesn't have the "Compare Grocery Carts" button to show a table of the items across all the grocery stores. This was the primary feature I was excited about, and without that feature, the app doesn't provide enough unique utility to be worth it for me.

super helpful for keeping on top of things we have in the pantry, making shopping lists, and meal planning! I am a little disappointed at the small amount of stores to choose from when making a shopping list, as none of those stores are really in the area I live in. this app has seriously helped me organize the kitchen though, and I'm so happy to report while using this app, I don't ever think I'll need to ask my partner "what do you want for dinner?" because the meal planning is so easy.

it's a good app, but it lags a fair bit more than I would expect, like every 3 new items I add or remove I can scroll and it'll take almost a full minute before I can add something else, while still being able to scroll. I do like the massive volume of recipes available tho, it's a variety from different cultures and difficulty levels. Love the concept and content of the app, just needs a tiny bit of adjusting membership price is kind of insane tho, $60 is a lot for any app tbh

Deceptive and wasted my time. It makes you go through the whole setup process. Picking your ingredients you have in your house. Choosing local grocery stores and linking those accounts. Just to get to the end of this lengthy process to tell you that is a PAID subscription. You need to tell people that upfront! On top of that it's $79 a year. Waste of time just to find out its a paid subscription. I would have considered it if the app was upfront a out the subscription.

Well I do love this app and it does have alot of cool features but im not paying $50 a year for it. Im tired of all these apps forcing you into subscriptions, they all add up and i can't afford it!! I was content with the free version until you made importing recipes a part of the pro only version. If I can't import then this app is no longer useful to me. I'll go back to using the free app whisk. I need to be able to import... uninstalled

Today I downloaded the app and spend a couple of hours trying the app. So far the free portion is good. I like that it let me list expiration date and able to add new products. However, I don't see anything that can let you filter items that are expired or about to be expired. As I toss out a lot of expired items I kept on thinking the app will be nicer if it let you filter by quantities on hand and expiration date and ability to suggest meal plans based on items about to be expired.

Honestly very unreliable. I've been subscribed for over 2 years and suddenly the app stops working right after I spent an hour setting up meals and shopping lists for the next 3 weeks. Can't access my lists or recipes. Now I have to start from scratch with pen and paper to go shopping while I pay for an unreliable service? Canceling if not fixed by end of today.

This app is perfect if youre willing to spend $60 (USD) per year. after signing up, I skipped subscribing, and was tempted by the follow up trial price of $1.99/month for the year. Still skipped subscribing to try the free version. all of the filters except for diet were blocked. However, I will compliment this app for the filter categories and recipes I could see. biggest feedback: at sign up, make adding stores optional. make adding at least 3 ingrdients required to continue

This app is extremely useful. It makes tracking all my consumable items (not just food, but other essentials like soap and such) a breeze, as well as figuring out where to shop for the best price. I've been using this app to plan meals for a few months now, and it has been great. Makes me feel like a chef, even though I don't actually know what I'm doing. The only nitpick I have is there is no search filter specifically for ingredients that are about to expire.

I am enjoying this app very much. I like that it populates my pantry items from my grocery purchases so I don't have to. It keeps track of my total spending in a pie chart by category, notifies me when things in my pantry are nearing expiration, and makes planning meals for the coming week much easier with recipes based on my pantry. Has a shopping list to make everything easy and fast. I like it so much I shared with my friends in my social media feeds.

Day 7, trial ended and I was billed. im only planned 1 week free to try it. Right now I'm trying to plan the week and build a grocery list. The app is very slow and locks up. I'm using a Samsung Galaxy Ultra which should have no issue. I'm also the CEO of a tech company so I'm familiar with software development. The app has a lot of potential to be better. It certainly can be faster it the data and API side was more efficient. The layout is very baggy, interacting with thr UI requires waiting. T

The app is amazing, I love the idea they have going. Very beneficial, the added featers are pretty neat as well. I gave it the rating I did because I did not like how I have to pay in order to save more than 3 receipts. Probably won't use this app as much as I would have because of that. I understand needing to make money off this, but if we could just save unlimitly without paying (like all other apps), then this would be a Gold Star app!

Great idea until you get to wanting to make something and it wants to charge you 60 bucks for a membership. It offers a week free trial but we all know how those go. Trying to cancel before the week is up is a pain. Good concept. Poor execution. I promptly uninstalled. I'm not paying 60 bucks for this, especially when you're already making ad money.

Created an account, too slow to log in. Wth?? I know my internet isn't the fastest, but this is ridiculous. 🤨 I waited for a good minute or two for my account to get set up, but all I could see is that "chef's hat" icon blinking on and off. Even my favorite games don't load this slowly. People don't have time for this kind of thing. How are they supposed to use the app if they can't even get into a newly created account? This needs to be fixed.

I love the concept, want to love it but... 1. It was slow. I spent hours trying to scan things into my pantry & it was buggy. 2. Pantry done. Trying to find recipes or templates was slow / didn't filter well for me. 3. Picked a template, pantry items didn't translate well. substituted things that wouldn't work just based on "chicken" being the ingredient. Canned chicken is not a chicken breast. 4. For the $$ I would want more than mobile. It wasn't user friendly and used lots of storage/battery

While there are tiny little "buggy" things going on, this is an amazing app. It took my wife and I about 2 weeks to get the hang of the flow, but now we're all in. Creating a meal plan and then having that convert to a shopping list that then sends to Walmart pick up is a game changer. I can't wait to see them add in other large chains like Aldi, Meijer, etc. Update: Gave 5 star, any issue I've had, customer service has responded to quickly. Deserves top rating. Love this app.

Hidden behind a pay wall really? I have seen ads about this, and honestly something that gives me a recipe based off what I have is exactly what I needed. I refuse to pay for this kind of service tho, as I set up my account its like we want you to try it. Pay $0.00 now, but it'll prolly be due later, or make me enter a card number to continue. That is my boomer complaint, if its a free trial or a service tell me before I waste my time. Because if I have to enter a card to use a "free" app.

Mostly good. My biggest complaint is when adding my own recipes, it often auto-assigns ingredients to the wrong step. Especially if it's referenced later. For example, One had placing cheese on it in one step, and melting it in another. The cheese was auto- assigned to the melting step, not the adding. Another recipe it kept placing the flour under combing wet/dry not the mixing stage. You can assign "unassigned" ingredients. There is no option to reassign an ingredient. Should add that

The paid version is nice, but I'm paying a lot for something I use rarely and just for the pantry. I'm disabled, so it's hard for me to cook, but it's nice to know what I have in my pantry. I keep it Just for that. However, the four stars is because I'm having sync issues with the grocery store I use often. As well as importing recipes into the application. Is this a known bug? Is there a fix in the works?

Where has this app been all my life?? I've tried so many meal kit services but this trumps them all. The price is outstanding compared to any meal kit service out there. I can cook recipes based on what I have in my pantry/fridge by selecting a little "cook now" tab! The AI recipe import feature is PHENOMENAL. I've been able to go through all my cookbooks and add my favorite recipes easily to my saved collections. ONLY issue I've experienced is the app is slightly buggy/slow on my S22 Ultra.

I simply wanted to order 1 product online, and I had to install this app to do it. Started a trail subscription hoping to order the product, except now I can't get to the product I saw on my web search. Horribly designed app, and I just want to end my trial right now, before credit card is charged. I can't find the option to end my trial. I better get that option before card is charged, or a complaint will be filed.

I like that the app connects to stores for price comparisons, lets me send my grocery list directly to a store cart, and lets me easily transfer past purchases to my pantry. But store connections are unstable, Jewel Osco has had an outage for weeks. Adding recipes is confusing, they appear in multiple collections automatically, and there's no way to import recipes from social media or at least add the recipe source. Pantry items show days since I added them instead of days until expiration.

apps great but want some small changes. Firstly, I would like to see a way to easily export my info in the future. +1 cause free does exist though still prefer the former,THX. they have the right wording around private information but I would like them to put this action behind it. secondly, I would like to be able to automatically transfer a specific ingredient, into a generic one. I would much rather my pantry said I had 12 eggs rather than 80%. they do this better than most, but could improve

The app is super easy to use, but I wish it was clearer before downloading that you needed to subscribe. I love the ability to inventory home goods and beauty products on top of groceries. Some grocery items are missing, but they're easy to add. Also, it's nice that it allows you to track how much of an item you have left by percentages. The only thing that would make it better is integration with myfitnesspal. Also, I wish there were more store options for the shopping function.

AMAZING TOOL! Especially for people who are budget and waste conscious. I OFTEN forget to scan in receipts after grocery shopping (which is an absolute A+ feature), but even manually updating my pantry 1-2 times a week takes WAY less time than meal planning before Cooklist. I've been really impressed with recipes database. Each one used has been delish and the "save" option for recipes to use in future is CLUTCH.

Great app overall, at least in the paid version. Only deducting one star because there's no integration with desktop or online. I find many recipes while using my laptop, and there's no way to save them so I can access them in the app later. I have to manually send the link to my phone, then copy it into the app. Big hassle, and there are other apps that are similar that DO have an online web program, usually a browser extension, for this reason.

Best app I've found to provide recipes I can make with the things I have at home. It provides substitutions, and my favorite is that you can put diet restrictions and food sensitivities, as well as the cooking medium, and it takes all of those things into account. Since going vegan and finding out I was allergic to soy & gluten & dairy intolerant, I've really struggled with meals and recipes. This app has made it so easy to find things to make,so I don't feel like I have limited eating options.

I just got this app and everytime I click on something I discover a new feature and fall more in love with this app! And I don't even have the premium version. No adds, price checking for local stores, shopping list, pantry list, experation reminders, meal planning, you can have your spouse/roommate/whoever linked as well to access what you have and the shopping list, recipes based off what you have in your pantry and even substatutes any missing ingredients with other items you have!! 10/10!

It keeps accidently selecting and then freezing on a recipe. Crashes every few minutes and has a long load time when searching for recipes. I would have loved it if it worked on my tablet but as it is I can't even get through a day. I might check it out again in the future if it improves because the idea of having an easy to use versatile tool for food planning is really appealing. In the meantime I must unsubscribe after 2 hours on a trial run

It has too many issues to be worth it, especially since it requires a subscription to do basic things. Can't input barcodes manually. Has trouble reading eggs or bar codes with white background. The user interface is laggy and that makes it hard to use. The account syncing can take days. Adding items from your receipt sometimes adds it to your shopping list instead. The only benefit it has over KitchenPal is the account integration, which is nice when it's working.

I paid for a subscription to access everything, and I'm very disappointed. The features are good ideas, but the interface is frustrating and clunky. It is so busy, searching for items or reading recipe directions is difficult. The recipe side is really lacking. Everything from storing recipes to listing the directions is just a little frustrating. For the pantry, scanning barcodes is great, but the shopping side is frustrating if you're adding additional items not from a recipe.

This app would be insanely more useful if there was a computer integration. I like having it available to reference if out and about in my pocket via the app but if I'm looking and finding recipes you see more on a computer verses your phone. Also, videos are easier to follow along with on a bigger screen. I'd say take our feed back seriously as I have seen I'm not the only one bringing this up and make it versatile. Then maybe it would be worth the pro version.

Overall pretty good. I like the "cook now" feature that suggests recipes for things I have on hand. Program doesn't realize fresh lemons are a substitute for lemon juice in recipes, and other odd things like that. Had to create my own workaround to track leftovers, which I think a pantry app should do more easily. Frustrated that I can't delete things from the shopping cart part of the app, only add to pantry. Free version of app mostly useless. Rating is for Paid version.

I love this app so much! I know what I have at all times, it helps me prevent wasting food and, so far, the recipes have all been amazing! I do have a few glitches, however. For example, it will tell me an item that I have removed is expiring, or have removed items listed in the "cook now" ingredients. Also, I periodically get logged out and have to log in several times before it will work. But, benefits far outweighs drawbacks. Love it!

Looove this app. It has many amazing features. I went shopping and never noticed I was overcharged until Cooklist added my items to the pantry. It gives recipes of foods I already have. Stores are synced to your account so you don't have to remember to scan receipts if you forget. You can make note of how much of an item you have left and it's expiration date. It is difficult to add items from recipes to the shop. Some of the words or buttons do not fit on the screen but it's no deal breaker.

The BEST meal prep app I've found by far! There is so many recipes to choose from! The barcode feature is one of my favorite features. It makes it super fast and super easy to add things to my pantry. You can even make your own grocery list on there and it will add up and estimate the total cost so you know how much you're going to be spending on groceries whenever you finally do go grocery shopping! There are so many more Amazing features those are just my favorites I definitely recommend!!!!

I love this app. Super easy to scan your pantry items into it. I love that it has recipes you can choose that sends missing items to your shopping list. Very cool and user friendly. One think that I think would be extra helpful is I wish that I could select an item from my pantry and have the app pull up recipes to try with that specific pantry item. Sometimes I will buy something that looks good in the moment and then forget how I wanted to use it in a recipe or not sure what to do with it.

I downloaded the app because it was advertised as easy to use. After navigating through the "try the free trial! We'll notify you before it's time to pay" area that is unskippable: You have to click through it and get up to agreeing for the free trial before the X appears where you can cancel out of it; you are then faced with linking your grocery store accounts. I tried navigating through it as well because I don't have any accounts to link, but the only option was to put in my zip code anyways

App lags so much. Constantly freezes when making changes to my pantry, updating food amounts/removing things I don't have anymore is infuriating. More than half of the time the app will not detect inputs until several seconds later. Sometimes, it doesn't detect them at all, completely unresponsive to clicks to remove an item. Extremely frustrating. Want to like the app, and it seemed good at first, but the cracks are showing and I don't know if I can continue using it until it's fixed.

This app is truly sensational. I get so many uses out of it, it is so easy to use. Very versatile. It changed my kitchen game (from none, to cooking for groups) I am very pleased with this app. Especially like the 'cook now' option that tells me what I can cook based on what ingredients I have in my pantry. It doesn't take too much time, free, no bothersome ads.

Some foods, like oatmeal, are missing from categories, so it's very hard to know that you have them unless you scroll through the All tab looking for them. This needs the option to add & assign our own categories. The Plan tab recommends recipes with food you don't have, but doesn't recommend food that is specifically listed in that category (ex. Breakfast bars). The barcode works really well, but if you can't tell what food you have, then the quick entry of food is irrelevant.

$65???? Ummmm, no. I haven't used it once, and can't find a number or contact info to reverse the money it took. UPDATE-I erased the last half of this post because of the swift action of Cooklist in refunding what was ultimately my error. I'm changing my rating to 5 stars due to the honorable way in which they dealt with my issue. Twin teenage daughters are expensive, else I would subscribe.

This is the best app I have found so far. The subscription fee is reasonable and definitely worth it. A widget would be super helpful, though. It needs computer integration because i use my laptop the most for finding recipes. A minimum on-hand feature would also be great! It connects automatically to some stores but needs a broader selection.

sadly this keeps bringing up a menu when searching recipes. it has a bookmark, shop, plan menu when I am simply trying to scroll. this is annoying however the bigger issue is the app is occasionally getting stuck. rendering it useless and I have to force close. the menu gets stuck open like that and then refuses to respond. a suggested feature would be to ask the user what cooking tools they have. not everyone uses an air fryer or pressure cooker. some people have rice cookers etc.

You have to physically connect to a outside vendor and have a profile already setup. They also rope you into a subscription after a 7 days free trial. Even though you are already paying for Sam's Club. I linked 2 accounts and they still did not display the items I wanted whenever viewing their web listings. Don't waste your time.

Not worth Pro subscription. The transfer to shopping cart doesnt work. Why cant I do cook mode while I'm in a recipe? Many, many duplicate recipes and the save recipe from website or TikTok only worked twice out of 5 tries. Pantry items that are virtually the same thing are stored as separate items. Example: fresh minced garlic, fresh garlic (which you can manually mince) and dried minced garlic. They should be interchangeable. Same with "pancake syrup" and maple syrup.

We're throughly in love with cooklist!! love all the features and it's helping us save so much money by tracking what we already have and giving substitutes for things I already have in my pantry to make recipes!! I love the expire soon feature and how I can filter allergies or food preference. I'm grateful that is also syncs up with instacart and I can sync up my purchases there and it even has non food things that carries over so I can better track my spending. Love You Cooklist Thank You!!🤗

Ingenious idea, but, as usual, all the good stuff is locked behind a subscription paywall. I know there's a free trial, but I don't want to subscribe just because I forgot to cancel the trial. Loosen up the free version (you could make it ad-supported), and I might reinstall. Heck, I might even pay.

love this app. I have been paying for pro for two years now. just recently switch grocery stores and having a hard time connecting with the new store. I attempted to import my digital receipt as screen shots using another phone... no can do. can you guys get us an update so the receipt scanning will recognize digital receipts? last time I recommended a feature you guys nailed it. (pantry scanning video/photos) seriously you guys amaze me thank you for everything!

Probably a good app, but as someone else pointed out, I can't try it without a trial for a paid service. You can't set up a household without connecting to a store account. 2 problems with that. 1, I don't know if I can trust your security. 2, you don't even have my store. why can't we just set it up without adding a store? sometimes I shop multiple stores for lowest cost and don't have accounts with all of them.

Edit: The speed issue mentioned below has gotten much better, but the connection to the various grocery store chains is, currently, quite buggy. Using that feature generally results in headaches. Original Review: Great concept, poorly executed. Very very slow. Changes to meal plans and shopping lists take minutes to populate. Probably won't continue after my trial expires

I usually don't rate this quick... the annual fee is nothing compared to how many fewer times I've had to go grocery shopping. The suggestions are great. The app developers thought of everything it seems. Just use that free trial. Get over the hump of adding stuff in (super easy to do and even add product that may not be on there.) And as an over stimulated single mom with raging unmedicated ADHD this app is a God sent.

Thanks for response and letting me know. The app is everything you want and then some, tells you recipes for your ingredients and also shows what can be supplemented with another similar item. The only thing is that the app is very slow, like laggy and some of the pop-ups are not responsive to me tapping the close pop-up button/X button and this forces me to close and reopen the app which really shouldn't happen.

Loving it so far! Thanks to my ADHD lack of object permanence and pantry cabinets that are far too deep, I frequently forget what we even have in there until I reorganize months later. This is great to help me keep an active, updated inventory of all food items in our house without me forgetting. 😅 HOWEVER, one downside is the recipe portion and allergies. When selecting food allergies, you can only pick the common ones. Mine are NOT, but are severe! Wish i could manually enter them.

The included AI tool that interprets even handwritten recipes scanned in with a phone camera is alone worth the subscription. I have many family recipes that I can now include in my meal planning and shopping lists. I can download a PDF of the recipes I've added, for safe keeping, should this app go bellyup eventually. I have used/tested MANY (many!) food/recipe apps over the years. This use of AI is a positive one IMHO. Thanks.

This was the exact app I was dreaming of. Everything is so convenient! I was tired of not using what I have in my pantry, this has reduced food waste and saved me money. It also has a nice feature that shows you recipes for what you already have. It also makes shopping way easier since if im at the store and forget if I have something, I can just check. As someone who has to keep the family fed and loves saving money, this app is a life saver! its also nice to not cook the same things over again

I was having no luck trying to contact the developer through the in-app contact form. My issue got resolved but after emailing them to the email they provided on pay store, theyve been trying to figure out why my app messages weren't reaching them. I really appreciated their responsiveness. It seems like they care a lot about the app and my experience on it. I changed my rating from 1 to 5 stars for that. The app does feel a little busier than I wish it was, but I'll give it a shot for a while

I love it. It helps me plan meals. Try NEW meals. I love the fact you can scan the barcode of product you have on hand. Shows you what you CAN make, with what you have on hand. And also what you might be missing. Gives you a good price range of what the cost of missing items will be, based on what store you pick to shop at.

I only just started, and I can't believe how useful and helpful this is! It is worth it for the convenience of knowing what I have at home and what I can do with it. I might forget a jar behind something in the fridge, but Cooklist won't forget! This app feels like an assistant in my phone who knows my fridge and pantry BETTER THAN EVEN MYSELF. 10/10 I would like to suggest a FEATURE. you should be able to see the tools/equipment needed/recommended for the meal (mixer/knife/blender)

What a waste of time downloading this app and going through the registration process. After skipping the free trial portion, as we all know how that goes, they only had 7 stores in my area listed, and only 1 of them I shop at, and it kept trying to download my purchases from there and wouldn't. Gonna look for a better app.

I have not fully utilized booklist yet. Hence just 4 stats not 5. That said I'm saving money already because the recipes make it a lot easier for me to cook at home. And with ingredients I already have. Plus it finds alternative ingredients when I'm missing them. That said, I expect to save more as I identify all my home inventory of food. And I expect to save even more once I setup online accounts with multiple grocery stores nearby.

I LOVE this app!! The meal planning, pantry items, and finding recipes that are based on whats in my pantry is a great idea however there are glitches that need to be fixed. Connection to the app keeps timing out, the weekly meal plan does not switch to the next week, today should be starting my new current plan schedule but it's still on last week's. Some of the substitutions are WAY off. When scrolling thru recipes it's 2 easy to select it, add, or delete by accident which is very aggravating

well, I love the idea of this app, and I do use it frequently. but it's clunky. I am paying for a service monthly that I cannot rely on. for instance. the pantry imports from stores are unreliable. I am only able to choose one Walmart that is recognized and is auto imported. it doesn't recognize the Walmart Market near my home. there is always an error so I have to enter items manually. the recipe important is glitchy, & it lags. this app needs a serious update and glitch fix.

Pantry tracker is OK but often doesn't register that you have the ingredients a recipe needs. Example: I manually add Schmaltz to the pantry, click on a recipe, and it shows "not in pantry: Schmaltz." Frequent freezes, slowdowns, and crashes when scrolling recipes. Cart sync is absolutely useless. It has a pathetic selection of stores; of the ones it does offer, the accounts don't work (target) or the app doesn't acknowledge your local store exists (food lion, costco, safeway).

Misleading advertising. "Contains in-app purchases" leads me to believe it's a free app with extra features you can pay for; however, this app costs money to even use beyond the first week. Plenty of websites can provide me with recipes based off what ingredients I input, but I thought an app would be more convenient.

Cooklist is the best meal-planning app I’ve used! It has seamless Walmart integration, so I can order groceries directly. The smart pantry tracking keeps me organized, and the meal generator suggests recipes based on what I already have, saving money and reducing waste. Other apps lack key features, but Cooklist combines everything perfectly. If you want an all-in-one solution for meal planning, grocery shopping, and pantry management, this is the app to get!

This is not free. I managed to be smart enough not to actually put a lot of work in the set up, maybe I've been burned enough. I was looking for something that might help me make more creative meals with limited ingredients and had hoped I found it. I just went in and entered some ingredients to test it out and it waited until the last step to tell me there was a free trial but it cost money to actually use. I'm glad I didn't waste more time on it.

I got this app thinking it was for recipes and looking through them. It immediately had me linking accounts to stores and then looking for ingredients. After I went threw that and went to look at recipes it immediately went into diet and health. Which I get it helps you narrow your list down to what you need but all before I could actively look made me exit out of the app and delete

It's been a great experience so far. The scroll function can sometimes be touchy thinking you picked a recipe. One thing I really wish it has was the ability to use it on a laptop or surface pro for desktop usage. Because I like seeing the recipe on a bigger screen instead of printing them all out. Other then that super useful and great at saving time!

The app has a lot of bugs for the paid functions. I cancelled since there are other free apps with the functionality that does actually work. It's not worth the $ since there are unable to keep stores connected for importing and the scan receipt function is an absolute joke. The frustrating thing is they know about the bugs when you report them and just tell you they are working on it. That's it. If I am paying for an app and it is known to be not working correctly I should not be charged.

I am a newly widowed mom trying to get used to my new normal I struggling financially having to go to food pantries, etc looking at almost bare cabinet / fridge googling struggle meals, etc. This app let's me make my nothings into something's. It's really helpful. When I get my food stamps and I save a recipe I want to try I love that I can create a shopping list associated with the exact recipe well actually the app does it for me. I am a visual learner so having videos etc perfect. Thank you.

So far I'm loving this app! One thing that would make it better is if it organized the shopping list by aisle numbers the items are in. I'm not sure if it's possible but if they know the price of items maybe they can figure out the aisle numbers. I spent a lot of time going up and down aisles trying to find items.

I really struggle with meal prep and planning, especially when it comes to remembering and using all the ingredients I have available. This app has SO MANY different approaches to helping me keep on top of weekly prep as well as making things on the fly with what I have. The initial setup was a little intense as I hyper focused on cleaning my kitchen and logging EVERYTHING, but now deciding what I can make and want to eat is so much simpler. Saves me mental energy and money!! Thank you!!

Just started using it and it has all the features I imagined and more. Use an app like Auto Clicker to share recipe links from apps like paprika for import. Love the purchase integrations and auto-renaming of recipe ingredients for inventory tracking. My only gripes are that the ui is cluttered due to all the features and likewise there are some bugs and occasional lag. Hoping it gets worked out, and once more stores have their carts integrated (WinCo, Vons) it will be usable for years to come.

This app is amazing!!! I love that it auto-shops and even calculates my total from my shopping list, and quickly plops it in my Target or Walmart cart for me to check out! And then it tracks what I bought to find me recipes with what I've got!! This app does everything I've ever wanted an app to do for my shopping list and meal planner!

Download beware! App looks free. It will have you scan all the stuff in your house. After you click done it brings you to their policy screen and 7 day free trial but it's $49.99 a year. I just wasted 30 minutes of my time to find out this is a yearly service fee? Very deceptive. Shame on you.

Very well thought out app. Couple of things, I can add photos of new items on my iPad app easily but Android phone will not let me open the camera. It will let me browse photos but when selected it doesn't actually import. Also when scanning, sometimes I inadvertently swing my hand across a partial label and it tries to add a new custom item but there is no button to say ignore this accidental scan.

A few minor annoyances here and there, not sure if they are app related or phone related (ie my camera won't load properly for barcode scanning) found some easy work arounds, so it's not a big deal. Love the macro break downs, it helps me meet my goals. Wish there were a few more grocery stores on the list. I live in the northern part of NH, we don't have Kroger's or anything like that. We have the IGA, Market Basket (Demoula's), BJ's Wholesale, Hannaford, and Shaw's 🤷. So far, loving it!!! ♥️

After trying to manually adjust the expiration date, the app froze. Tried restarting and froze again when entering the item in the pantry again. Uninstalled and tried again. Now when trying to login, it can't find my account. Deleted the app after only 5 minutes of use. Unfortunate since the app had everything I was looking for.

Love it! Meal planning is a breeze with the feature. Helps keep me on track with time, financial, and health goals. The availabilty of so many different recipes keeps the palate from boredom while pursuing goals. I used it reviously on my Lg app.I would have given 5 stars but my order did not sync to the cart in the Walmart app so had to manually add each item. Also, staple pantry ingredients such as salt, pepper, and oil should populate differently where you check only if needed.

I've been using sidechef for years now and while running into the occasional issue it was never unusable. However the past few weeks the recipes no longer save into the grocery list which causes a lot of back and forth through individual recipes to figure out what I need. I've tried to delete and reinstall the app with no success. I'm hoping this can get fixed soon!

Truly one of the best recipe apps I have used and during stay-at-home, I have tried a lot and none come close to sidechef. So many great recipes that can be curated by levels of difficulty, ingredients, cuisine, dish type, diet, etc. I've only used the free version and it's impressive. My only minor suggestion is if you could customize your grocery store. Currently it only offers Walmart, which is not my preferred store. Shout-out to HEB!

I do like the assessment phase of your app. where you choose a specific diet, and your food prefrences. Develop that further to include other diets. But when entering a recipe of your own creation, it does not allow a range of food choices. such as, "1 cup chopped ham, crumbled bacon or breakfast sausage". it only allows the selection of one ingredient. And there is no place to save the instructions for the recipe, which leaves the recipe incomplete. Only for a very basic list of ingredients.

As a full time mom and a full time employee, this app has been a life saver on extra busy weeks. I can access the recipes from a computer or my phone, adjust for number of servings, uncheck what I don't need, and send the list to my store. They shop for me and bring everything to my trunk. I get to spend the time I would be spending at the store interacting with my kids and cooking something new for them to try. They're so much more willing to try new things. Love this app.

I love the recipes, collections, and articles that come from this app. With the addition of meal plans, shopping list, and shopping from Amazon features, there is no way I couldn't give it less than 5 stars. I can't wait to see what other quality of life changes they make in the future. Maybe a serving size adjuster? Edit: Don't know how I missed that! I do see the serving size adjuster now. Thank you for the quick reply!

This isn't a meal planner. For some reason you have to sign up. After that you are given the option of what you would like from the app, meal planning, eating on a budget, etc. Then you can tell them food allergies and aversions. So I checked nuts (allergy) and mushroom (just don't like them) One of the first suggested recipes is something with mushrooms and pistachios. This is just another recipe app, which can be found anywhere on the internet.

Love this app it makes cooking and shopping so much easier. Hours spent at the store have turned to 10 minutes for pickups. I don't have to search for recipes then write it all down and check off a list. Just pick the recipes I want then order them. Awesome I was so surprised thus app is free!! I've recommended it to plenty of family and friends truly a time and money saver.

So helpful! Has revolutionized my approach to cooking/groceries and empowered me to grow! One thing that I would love to see is the ability to add your own notes to saved recipes. "Husband thought it needed more seasoning", "Add extra beans", "Great leftovers!" etc. Would be very helpful when you make a recipe again. Absolutely terrific app 👍🏼

Just accessing the app for the first time now. Seems to be useful and functional. So far, I really like the ability to create easy-to-reference grocery lists. Can't wait to get shopping and cooking! Will update rating with use. A few suggestions: - I'd love to see more slow cooker recipes. - When removing an item from the shopping list, I wish there was an "Undo" feature. - I wish I could add web links to recipes I find elsewhere to my meal plan. - Any plans to work with Instacart?

I love this app! It took awhile to get used to but once I changed my mind set it's very good. I usually shop a couple of times a week, and other apps have forced me into saving all the ingredients into the grocery list once you make your weekly plan. You can still do that, but this app will let you just put the days you will be shopping for into the list. The format of the recipes I think needs some work but that may just be me

The ingredients listed to add to shopping cart aren't the same as the ones in the recipe. I get that they want to partner with Walmart or whatever, but a pork loin chop isn't the lamb chop in the literal title of the recipe. Just let us make lists of ingredients instead of adding to a cart. I can do that on my own. You're making a convenience inconvenient.

I love the idea and the features, some features I'd like to see added, expand the ingredients to avoid so you can add your own (like beef liver, something we'll never use or sesame seeds because someone can't eat them). a way to dislike recipes. not rate them, but just hide them from your results all together.

Your app is okay but for me it is missing some key features. It would be nice to have a pantry where I could add the items that I already have into a list as well as I details like expiration dates to keep my produce fresh. Possibly suggest receipes with the ingredients I have. Also I noticed that unless the ingredients is worded correctly or on your list you cannot save it which I don't like as sometimes I use ingredients that are not available on your app.

Best app for meal planning, finding new recipes or finding your old favorites. Lots of options to filter recipes for different diets or eating styles. As well as recipes that contain your allergens. You enter your favorites and it makes suggestions based off all your preferences. It also sets goals about whether you want to learn to cook, learn to cook on a budget, or eat healthy.

I have tried multiple cooking app. I have enjoyed many of them. This app however is my favorite. It does a lot of things other apps also do, only better. The meal planning, shopping list, recepe books and ways of finding fun recipes are all wonderful and better than any other cooking app. Even the format to reading the recipe is different, easier, and over all better than any other app I have tried. I have so much I want to say about this app but am out of room so just try it for yourself!

There's a lot of variety in the types of recipes but very few options for customizing your diet preferences which makes it hard to hunt through when there are a lot of foods you can't or won't eat. For example, you can't tell the app you don't like salads or spicy food or lentils, etc. Also the options they do give you don't block out every recipe with your allergens. For example I told the app I am soy-free but several recipes with edamame or tofu have shown up when I browse through recipes.

I love this app for its recommendations on food and the ability to find low cost meals per serving. Price checking with local Walmarts is an added benefit. I would have given SideChef 5 stars with the pantry sync and ability to find recipes based on ingredients you currently have. However I have run into one bug so far and that is the meal planning not loading at all. Adding name brand items to your pantry can be a pain but all that matters truly is that you know they are there.

Love the app. Please continue devs. Would love feature to specify order date. Another feature request: if I could see my in–cart ingredients with the option to group them by recipe, by ingredient number, cuisine, or by day of grocery ordering. Last grade–A feature is to link nutritional information of ingredients to the ingredients/recipes (maybe even aggregates for recipes) for easy lookup. That'd be a game changer for me. Recipes should be more editted & extensive in #. Be more open w/ info!

Not a good meal planning app. Black pepper can be in 4 recipes, and end up in your cart 4 times. Not any clear way to condense like items. Not any clear way to mark items as in your pantry. Seems to add items to my cart even if unchecked,? Browsing recipes is also a mess, doesn't feel good. Seems to be a Walmart companion app but doesn't consider if my store would even have something in stock (fresh herbs, collards, special seasonings, grains). 2 stars for stability + not blowing up my phone

I love the concept so much! I hate meal planning and getting my grocery list ready and this app makes it so easy. However, this app will not load my grocery list that I generated for my week of meals, no matter how long I give it. It's very slow and lags after selecting items.

So far I've had no issues. I've yet to actually order ingredients and have them sent to me but just the concept of the app is extremely helpful, I love the multiple healthy food options especially small snack and breakfast items, I always struggled with finding simple healthy things to eat in the morning and this app gave me so many helpful ideas.

I got this app due to another one "stopping" and recommending this one. My problem is that they don't function the same at all. This is probably best when you know what you're cooking and don't deviate. As a person who wants more inspiration and less "create a shopping list", this didn't work for me. Maybe I need to spend more time with it, or try to work out how it could work for me. Either way it wasn't intuitive, nor did it fulfill the purpose I downloaded it for.

On mobile, I love this app. On my chromebook it keeps crashing. I would prefer to use chromebook because its easier to setup in the kitchen to follow directions and see the videos instead of squinting at my phone the whole time. Please look into getting this sorted. I would really love to be able to keep utilizing this experience.

I love this app - the free version is super robust and has everything I was looking for: ability to search by ingredients or diet preferences for meals, easy to create a meal plan, and transfers all ingredients to a grocery list. Saves me a ton of time. Only thing missing is someone to cook the meals I choose!

I've used and loved Budgetbytes for years. SideChef actually has some cool tools but it's apparently just a big shill for Walmart now?? I can't choose my actual grocery store, even though they're much closer to me than any Walmart. (They're also a major chain that offers all the exact same options for pickup/delivery as Walmart.) Giving it a shot, but it's SO obnoxious- will probably uninstall soon.

Convenient to pull things together and send your grocery list over. I like that you can create different favorites folders. However, Mealimes ability to suggest plans with consideration to food waste is definitely a step up, but you will find a wider variety of recipes here. The recipe import is extremely annoying b/c I have to manually input instructions or it just links backs to a website that you have to fight blog posts and adds to view. The entire multipage print out ads are absurd.

5-star features, like the meal planning calendar and the step-by-step recipes, make this a super helpful & useful cooking app!...but it's slowness is frustrating. It takes longer than it should for each new screen to load and the transitions in general are way too long. In a vacuum it's doable, but when actually using the app it just feels really sluggish. Also, I can't figure out how to remove meals from a plan? It's features keep it at 3*, in hopes that the issues will be ironed out soon.

great app although some small things [that i can let go] did annoy me like how i selected some foods [from the start menu when you first install it] of ingredients i disliked and it seemed to 'push' some meals with those ingredients to my front page [yes i can alter meals but still was a little annoying] friendly UI & good if you want to find / try something new, and id say this was a small issue [for me at least] was the step by step i structions, some felt very vague while some helped. 4/5 <3

Great app that helps with finding recipes, planning meals and creating shopping lists. I like that you get the option to create your own recipes too, and also choose which ingredients in the recipes you want to add to your shopping list. Just a couple of issues that make it annoying sometimes: choosing dates in meal plan, and the app freezes sometimes.

Requires users to create yet another account with yet another company. For the money you will spend on this ripoffware over the years in terms of recurring subscription fees (there is no possibility to purchase the app once and for all), one could buy a dozen good books -- sure, they won't contain a million run-of-the-mill, cheaper-by-the-dozen variations on a theme, but 100 original, sophisicated, high-quality menus instead of 1 mio. mediocre simple plates will taste a lot better. Uninstalled.

There's a good variety of recipes available, but I wish the cookbook function were a bit more intuitive. Something like, when you go to add a recipe to a cookbook, the list of cookbooks has a checkbox next to each one. That way, you can easily add or remove a recipe to and from multiple cookbooks at once and also see which cookbooks already have that recipe.

Great for meal planning! I love that you can put in your own recipes so I can quickly add my go to recipes to my shopping list, and then go in and check off anything I already have. I love that it sorts the list automatically. Some things it doesn't have, but almost everything is there and if its not you can put it in manually, it will just be in an other category. Thats how I add my cleaning supplies and such that I need from the grocery store. Super helpful app, one of my favorites for sure!

So far really nice. I would however like the option to stop seeing certain styles of food - the app is loaded with sweets and desserts, and I'm really not interested in them. I'm looking for healthy meals. You can already specify dietary requirements and receive tailored recommendations, which is good, I'd just like that expanded a little please!

A good app and with great recipes. But there are some improvements to be made: 1. The quantities are not always consistent between metric and imperial and can be confusing to read. What is 4pckg of white sugar? 2. The app notifies me constantly when I lose connection. This would be fine. But, it happens when I'm not using the app as well as when it's in the background which is very distracting. Please turn this off. 3. I want compete freedom to add my own weird ingredients to the shopping list.

I love this app, it's my favorite recipe/planner app. Easily create shopping lists and add or remove ingredients. Prepare the weeks food ahead of time with the meal planner. Lots of great recipes. The only problem, and it drives me crazy, is a serious amount of lag when pressing buttons. Each time I want to go back or forward or each new screen takes a second or two to load. It doesn't sound like a lot but it adds up and becomes quite frustrating. I have a Note 10+ and LTE data so it's not me.

It's a great concept and execution. I'd love to see the grocery list get some more functionality. As a very simply list, it's relatively easy to add the ability to edit or remove individual list entries, so unfortunate you can only check them off or clear the whole list. I haven't explored more than one or two recipes, but the ones I've seen have been very clear and attractive layouts and instructions. Will for sure use it for my meal planning, but a better list would be 💯.

App is awesome but please create a one time/lifetime payment option. Currently deciding on a cooking app to commit to and pay for and the only thing stopping me is the recurring yearly subscription of $87 AUD. If a lifetime purchase option was provided (given that it would be able to sync to new devices via email log in) I would definitely choose this one.

Very good app for beginner cooks or experimented ones that want to try something new, with defined steps and basically all the information you want (ex.g.:Time,portions,diets,carbs/cals/workout) anything will fit the bill on what you are trying to eat in the near future. My favorite feature is the ingredient search, being a student, on the last day of the month there aren't many things to choose from, but I can search the ingredients I still have and cook a restaurant graded dish I will enjoy.

*EDIT Updated review to 3 stars because of fantastic customer service. Thank you to Jimmy for helping me with a workaround for searching Annoyed within 2 minutes of opening the app - the allergies option should either include more options or give something like a dropdown menu or an option for a list of ingredients to block. Just because my allergies are not common does not mean they're not valid, and i don't want to have to hunt through the menu options to try and find something that i can eat

I hope that this app was for me. The meal planner is practical, and neat. This app also offered lots of recipes, even they can add the ingredients and turn it into shopping list. But apparently it's inappropriate for someone who lives outside US and wants to cook different kind of cuisine, Japanese food or Korean dish for example. I can't add personalised ingredients and save it to the ingredients list when I want to add my own meal recipe in the meal planner. Had to uninstall this, sorry.

My favourite free recipe app, its filters are thorough and they work. I rarely have to wade through recipes I don't want to find something I do. It's also well curated, rather than bombarding you with random guy's cheesy dross like some other apps. However, even with notifications switched off, it still bombards my phone. I've had to block it from doing so manually. Please fix that, some of us don't want a recipe app to be yelling every couple of hours.

This is not a full review. I just downloaded this app. It seems very promising. Set up did not take much time but once I started adding recipes to the meal plan, things started to get a bit strange. None of the recipes I added appeared on the meal planner. After some time playing with the app and the shopping list to figure it out, the recipes started appearing on the meal plan so the app was just too slow. I will continue to use the app as it does seem to b really promising. updates to follow.

App itself is good but the notifications are dreadful. They are just too many, and they're repetitive, not actually for anything other than "hey, open the app". They seem to have got worse recently, and despite me unticking in app settings I still get notifications that I don't want. Have had to block the whole app's notifications through my phone.

Been using this app for a while and mostly it's extremely useful and user-friendly. On occasion it can be difficult to navigate, as one problem I had was the ingredient search. It has a "recipe search" bar and an option for ingredients, however, it was not clear to me that you could just search "[ingredient]" in the recipe search bar and it would show you recipes with that in it—I thought you could only search ingredients in the ingredients section (and in that the choices are limited).

This is probably fine as a recipe inspiration app, but I wanted a meal plan/shopping list app and I can't recommend this. Issues include; - Import of recipes is very hit and miss. I tried from 6 different food websites and 4 didn't work. - App is SLOW, and you can't add multiple ingredients to a recipe or list at once - No way of moving ingredients between categories, so you end up going backwards and forwards round the supermarket because things are listed in different sections Character limit

I downloaded a bunch of cooking apps, this one was the best. It pretty much has everything you could ask for except the nutritional value, but I think its still worth it. Not only it has the basic shopping list/cookbook/ meal planner and a lot of categories thing that many cooking apps do. IMO sidechef has the nicest interface and a lot of other features. IT HAS AN INGREDIENTS WIKI! it tells you the benefits, ways to prepare it, how to buy it, etc. Also, I live for its meal plans and colections!

I can't honestly explain how much I love this app. I uninstalled it because it was running pretty slow for me but after testing other apps I feel like nothing compares to this one. The recipes are amazing and look I'm not even a cooking person, I can only make basic stuff out of the top of my head. I have a hard time eating healthy too so the fact that I can plan meals ahead and add everything to a shopping least is just perfect. Again, it just runs really slow but 100% worth it.

It's a pretty cool app. Has good access to recipes and it is nice to be able to add the incredients from it to your cart. Recipes need more variety. I see alot of the same ones in recommended and trending. They should update more regularly or be able to shuffle. Writing your own recipes is kind of useless. Why can't you add steps to your recipe, only ingredients?

Would have been great of it wasn't so buggy and lacking some essential features. I'm cooking for just me so I just need meals to serve 1-2. While I can adjust this when I go into the recipe, I can't save that serving size so when I try to make a shopping list, it gives me the amount for the default serving of the recipe. When I try to change the serving size then save that individually as a shopping list, I can't access the list.

Won't create shopping list from meal plan? *Edit - all fixed - just had to close and reopen the app 🙈 - Would be great if you could make the meal plans collaborative though? - And the shopping list - maybe there could be a function to upload a list of everything you already had in your cupboard, so that these would automatically be taken off, shortening the list? - Then maybe you could search recipes for ingredients which you already had? Many thanks ☺️

Great shopping and recipe planning app. A lot of good recipes, some of which are gated by a dollar paywall, though you can see what it is before deciding if it's worth it or not. Ton of free recipes that I've cooked many of, and the app can double as a personal shopping list as well. Overall, great get.

they added a subscription feature. also kinda stupid when you think about it. I just activated my free trial, took a bunch of screenshots and canceled it. now when I need more I just start a free trial with a different email😂😂

I have just started using this app and I must say, I really like it. I love the ease of converting to Walmart for ingredient purchases. Which blends perfectly with Walmart's home delivery service. I love the simple but very well-put-together details of the app. The only ask is that you guys add general grocery lists based on type i.e. Paleo, keto, etc, and connect those lists to Walmart for kitchen basic staples needed. Thank you

I really enjoy the app - Unlike pinterest, I can look at a recipe and not have to skim through an entire blog just to find the ingredients. It's easy to save recipes. But finding relative content is not as easy. At first it was great, but now I only see the types of meals I would never make.

Very good recipe app. Lots of quality food recipes can be found on here. Some requests that I have for you to add in: -A "recently added" feature so that you can see what recipes add new. -A "random recipe" feature would be awesome as well if ou aren't entirely sure what you want to make. Thanks guys for all your hard work in making an app like this.

I really enjoy using this app to make my weekly meal plan. I think this is the only app can fulfill what I need - meal plan, shopping list and my own cookbook in the same app. Still there is something if improve, it will be a perfect app: 1. Editable ingredient and measurement 2. Can add your own recipes to your cookbook (it is so disappointed that I can have my own cookbook but without my own recipes !!) 3. Number of serves per recipe (then muliply the amount of ingredient if I need more serves

i love how simple the ui is, the ingredient nutritional info pages are really great too, i appreciate the no bs approach. i just have a few suggestions. when i added ingredients to my pantry, i expected to be able to also enter the amount i have, so that the app would know to not add to cart what i already have if its enough for the recipe, or at least have a little disclaimer that i do have that ingredient. would love that feature in the future! also the ingredient list could be improved too 🩷

I like the app and it has some good recipes, but it won't stop suggesting vegan recipes. I have my settings set to "no diet" but 80% of the recipes I get are vegan. There should either be an options to choose "no vegan" or it shouldn't just suggest it to you without selecting it. Very annoying.

I've had this app for yrs. After an update months ago I lost all my saved recipes. Thousands gone. Ever since then when I try to add more it saves the recipe but when I go to look at my saved items nothing actually gets saved. Before this issue I absolutely loved the app. Hopefully this can be fixed. I want to love this app again.

Cannot delete anything. Cannot change settings for units either. Stupid that you cannot choose saved recipies in specified cookbook for meal plans, you have to scroll though all recipies. Would have been more convenient to add prices for automatic calculations to keep track of budget as you Shop.

I'm on the fence about this app. I'm learning to cook. I have ADHD and I saw this after learning that Blue Apron has step-by-step cooking checklists to help the less-kitchen-oriented... So far... I can't say I'm learning much. Not many of the recipes are thorough enough for beginning cooks - more than a few skipped steps. I, also, am hesitant to try the grocery developer

This app is so weird?? I can't delete stuff I accidentally put in my meal plan, I have to put my type of diet every time I search in the meal plan, I don't have the possibility to check my type of diet when NOT searching in the meal plan, I can't read the nutritional information and calories of the recipe- I have to calculate it myself if I really want to (which is something very important)... This app have great potential but those really put me off wanting to use it... Please fix those.

I like the app but I wish I could add my own ingredients to my meal planner. When adding one of my own meals to the planner, I have to choose ingredients from a list and if my ingredient isn't there then I'm screwed! For instance, I'm planning a Katsu Chicken Curry dinner, the Katsu seasoning I use isn't on the SideChef list, therefore I can only add chicken and rice to my meal!

Please make units editable so we can add custom units such as portions. Also let us add our own ingredients. Couldn't put in recipe because I can only add Italian Dressing, but not fat-free Italian dressing. It's not like your app has listed every possible ingredient. If you can't customize, it's useless.

I like the app and it has alot of good recipes. I would like to add recipes from scratch as well as from other online sources. I love the calendar with the option to use a plan generator. I would like a way to share with another household member using the app to help with shopping lists, meal plans and cooking for the week.

I like it! I LOVE the shopping list feature and how it organizes items by category and you can check them off while you shop. I wish there were more comments/ reviews under recipes though. Sometimes it's nice to get other people's suggestions but I'm sure more will come with time.

The recipes that I've tried so far were ok, but it's annoying that you can only order from Walmart. Also, I think that this app focuses a little too much on quantity over quality. I could tell by reading through some of the recipes that they were junky blog recipes

This app far exceeded my expectations! I love it. You get tons of recipes in a huge selection of categories. The recipes are presented in amazing step by step videos so anyone can cook them. You can create your own cookbook, and it comes with 5 different voices to choose from for the narrator of the video. What more could you want?

This app is not what Budget bytes used to be, and I'm quite sad about that. I would cook anything B.B. puts together, but this is just a ridiculous conglomeration of tonnes of creators. More does not equal better. Searching is terrible, and it's not as time saving anymore. Now I have to go to the budget bytes site to get specifically her recipes, then search for what her recipe is called in sidechef and many times her recipe doesn't even come up. I'm just so frustrated with this app. 0 stars

Super great source of recipes. has step by step mode to walk you though which is handy. I hate how with my samsung note, there is no intuitive way to back a screen. I have to close program to get to recipes again. I get stuck in cookbook, get stuck in meal plan. its really frustrating. could be a wonderful app but I may end up deleting it.

The app reads the clipboard on startup. Besides that it seems good. You get recipes, can switch from imperial to metric for quantities. Then there's the possibility to schedule somwhere in the week and export the lacking ingredients to a shopping list (with quantities only in imperial, unfortunately for Europe). The 1 star rating is for the app accessing the clipboard.

I love this app! Where have you been all my life! :) the design is so clean, and the way the meal plan and the shopping list is connected is a really good idea. The intuitive recipes feed is really nice too so you can focus on your diet. Hopefully they add a Snack option in the Meal Plan page.

I want to like this app, but it very much pushes form over function. Great photos, navigation is slick, but I can't find what I want. Case in point, the splash screen offers meal planning and other really cool content. But once that goes away, it all vanishes and I'm left to search only recipes. That's cool, but thats probably the one thing this app has over Pinterest

Like the concept of recipe curation and planning and even bringing in the option to do grocery ordering from the app to suit your list of recipes. I didn't like that the only grocery partnership offered in my area was for Walmart, would have preferred a greater diversity of grocery fulfillment options.

What a great app! Very comfortable to use, and always handy. Love it, really, I've been looking for something like this for a lot of time. The best thing is how easy it is to use. The app design makes everything look nice and tidy, with a lot of well-ordered recipes to try. But that's not everything. You can have your recipe ingredients added to your shopping list and organized for everyday meals in an inside calendar! Just great.

I like the app very much. It's what I've been looking for. I believe this app will benefit if a online import recipe feature could be added. I'm sure I'm not the only one who has lots of bookmarks of recipes, and adding ingredient by ingredient is very time consuming. Nevertheless a big thanks to the creators!

I like the UI if the app, yea it is a little laggy but thats ok (for now). 3 things i would like to see in here is a way you can easily delete a shopping list, sync the meal plan with another user ( wife and hubby for example) and finally Echo show intergration. If it had all of these i would pay 10 bucks for this app..edit i just found out slide to left to delete lists

I love the functionality of this app, although I'm having trouble adding recipes. So far the only way I can add recipes is if I put them directly into the meal planner, but I really just want to add my recipes, then put them in the planner so it's all ready to go later on. It sounds tedious but this would work so much better.

Update: Scrolling issues have been fixed. The client runs much smoother now. It's a pity that you have to make an account even to use favorites but that's not that much of an issue anyway. [Review updated to 5 🌟] Laggy as hell. Using it equals to torture. Would be willing to give it another try if it performed better.

I couldn't even open it after installing. It kept force closing which is disappointing because I thought this app had a lot of features that could be helpful for meal planning. I should mention that I did add this as an app for my chromebook and perhaps this isn't optimized for it and is for a phone or tablet.

I am not THAT accustomed to cooking apps. This one is amazing. You can adjust the servings instead of having to calculate the difference yourself. I love that it shows me step by step with pictures or videos. Awesome recipes. Got some great butternut squash recipes. So excited! Visually appealing graphics and design.

Love! Love! Love! You can add your own recipes or choose from the many wonderful looking ones already in the app. Storing your recipes makes it easy to meal plan especially since SideChef generates a shopping list for you based on the ingredients you add. Highly recommend.

this is the best meal plan app I have ever downloaded- it took me a very long time to find a good one that didn't make me pay for everything. thank you so much. I think the people who couldn't open it didn't have an updated phone. mine is a Samsung Galaxy J3 v but it works perfectly. also, thank you for making it ad free. God bless your efforts!!!😍

Not as good as budget bytes. This app could have easily migrated some of the fabulous recipes over there and now it's mediocre at best. I'm so disappointed. Also some ard marked premium now so I'm sure there is going to be a cost but cannot tell how much anywhere.

Great for recipe discovery and meal planning. Love that I can swap recipes out and get new suggestions. Wish I could have more control over what stores I can shop. App navigation broken after latest updates. Can't navigate using the main navbar or tap into recipes.

I'm glad to see an active development team here. I had to give a three star rating for the app due to it's performance issues but I'm glad to say that the issues are now gone. The app is a great companion for a home cook. I love the ability to filter specific style of recipes. For me as an acrive gym goer those would be the high-protein recipes. When I have a vegan guest, it's so simple to just browse a vegan recipe. Also the quality of the recipes is great and consistent.

This is an excellent app. I love the filters, number of recipes is amazing! I love the diversity, photography, and the option to create your own cookbook. I like that you can make your own grocery list and filter ingredients too! It's not often that I rate an app, but this really is a good one.

Seems like really good app, ncie UI and photos but when i want to add stuff to my pantry I am not able to scroll down in list of ingredients. I can type one by one and it'll find it but scroll down so not happy as I'm not going to type hundreds of stuff one by one

Best meal plan app ever!!!! Purchase of pro content is based on bundles of recipes, so you dont pay for what you don't need, and it's super easy to get a recipe you really want without committing to a monthly subscription! Best!

Great app, very well made flow wise, but the biggest issue is scrolling through the receipes...it is sooo slow. Slow to a point that if I dont find a decent receipe in the first 10 or so I bail and change apps. Using a Pixel 3 XL..should be able to handle a simple scroll of a page.
