This is the best no nonsense workout tracker I have seen . Simple and easier than using a notebook if u just want to keep track of workouts and progression of each exercise. Fast and easy to enter info or change order of exercises during your workout. Its free and works so well that after using it for a while you will feel guilty if you dont donate something. No ads no popups. It just works day after day. And easy to add exercises or create different workouts for different days.

I don't think there is a better app out there that is as easy to use than this!  I have been using it for a long time and I love it! Simple, straight forward, and i recently discovered that my data can be backed up which was a life saver when I got a new phone.  I often feel sorry for people who still use a piece of paper to track their progress at the gym because almost everyone I see has a smart phone on them and they could save the paper and keep everything tracked wherever they go!

Easily the best fitness app out there. There is no secret pay wall trickery in this app and it does so much for free. You can add any workout you can think of and it will show graphs of your progress. The user interface might be a bit intimidating at first, but once you learn it you will love what this app has to offer. And unlike every other fitness app, there are no ads on this one!

This is a fantastic app, the best fitness tracker I've used. It's so clean, shows no ads, rarely recommends pro. Just overall fantastic. I've been using it for 3onths and I'm still learning what it can do, because the basic settings require no learning, just easy input. It's great for just remembering what your last set was or what exercises you did the session before. Great app for any level of gym go-er.

Can already tell this exactly what I was searching for. Simple to use, data collection for your work outs. Easily see what day of the month you did what exercise. And you can create your own entry for new/ unique work outs. I like to use this to make sure I do my daily planks. And now I'm adding my upper body work outs too it as well. It's honestly perfect.

I've been using this app for almost 7 years now I think. It's the only app I use cause I can create custom exercise, add notes, easily look back at past PRs. I almost switched to iPhone but got sad that I'd lose fit notes data, so I tossed the iPhone and went back to android after 3 days.. Suggestion though for loyal users. Could you guys add workout templates? It be great to create my own templates and use them whenever I need them. This would help tremendously.

This is hands down the best workout tracker I have used. Besides having an awesome sleek look, there is so much depth to this app if you are serious about tracking workout details from something as small as a single repetition to long term, yearly trends. The sheer customizability and organization is absolutely incredible, and the developer is constantly trying to refine this amazing product. This has absolutely changed the game for my workouts.

Excellent no bs app. However, it lacks a way to export or back up data. Pros: simplicity, fast, no account require, no ads, no time wasting bloat. Cons: no way to import or export data, data gone if switching devices- only needs a backup method to gain 5 stars. Wouldn't mind paying a few bucks to buy a premium version that can do this. Who is it for: Experienced lifters. New lifters may need an app that demonstrates the lift. That being said, this is nearly perfect for seasoned lifters.

Love how intuitive this app is to use when tracking and planning my workouts. Feature request: on the plate calculator, please add a percentage field. I would like to enter a weight, enter a percentage (e.g. "85" %), and be able to see what weights to load onto the bar. This would prevent me from switching back and forth from this app and my calculator. My coach gives me workouts like "4 sets of 5 reps at 60% of 1RM, 1 set of 3 reps at 80% of 1RM" and this would make early mornings easier.

In summary, this app is everything you need with none of the bloat. Simple, customizable, and all about the user. There's no useless graphics, transitions, or menus and no garbage to sell you because let's be real - we go to the gym to workout and don't want to waste time navigating interfaces needlessly. Fantastic app.

This was exactly what I was looking for to record my workouts. I was always a paper and pencil guy writing in my notebook for the gym but this has completely replaced that. It's easy to use. Got no ads. I'm able to look at my workouts from a calendar view and easily go into each workout to see what I did before. Can't believe it's free honestly.

I had a great workout app on my iPhone that I loved but unfortunately they didn't have an Android version and I tried about 6 or 7 different apps on Android finding all of them to either be too expensive, to complicated, or too focused on coaching and muscle groups and all of that nonsense. this is the only one that is useful in its free version and is relatively simple and straightforward. it's definitely not as intuitive as the app I had on my iPhone but it's the closest one I've found.

A great fitness app. Love seeing the graphs of lifts and the breakdown of parts of the body hit by the exercises. The use of this app alone has allowed me to push my self harder and get better lifts. It is great to see the past data and get over the psychological barrier of a lift when you know you have the data to back up the jump in weight. Overall phenomenal app and I'm super happy with it

Using it for many months Gave donation Simple to log exercises Great that you can export to spreadsheet Maybe someday a new product comes along that i want to use so i can export past data to. I still have my hand written college exercise notes Now no more paper. Great that it doesnt have a bunch of videos that clutter up things Navigation a little bit confusing. Would like included a Run Keeper module Best app that does it the way i like, simple.

This app is fully-featured, intuitive, and actually saves time in the gym. It's got timers that restart not just every X minutes, but as soon as you log a set, meaning that your rest intervals are accurate. It keeps useful statistics, and a calendar shows you what categories of exercise you do when. The dev has been upgrading and maintaining it for years. Great app.

Been using the app since 2014, just paid today to support. Best app I've used for tracking all aspects of my workouts. I wish there was a feature to let you share a complete workout that you've built, vs a single day. That would be an amazing feature for personal trainers too, as well as a good way to propagate the app. Another nice feature to have would be the ability to link with other users.

This app is quietly genius. It may not appear flashy, but I've found it provides a flexible framework to track my fitness activities without rigid restrictions that caused me to uninstall similar apps. The workout routines are a fantastic time-saving feature, not only for recording, but also deciding what exercises to do today. Bravo!

Best weight workout tracker hands down. No BS in app purchases or ads. Has a great variety of built in workouts, and if yours isn't showing up you can add it to the database. Has a thorough stats tracker so you can compare yourself to past workouts. Has a built in timer and weight log. You can customise the weight increment on a per workout basis, which is great when you have a collection of both lb and kg plates. It does everything I ever need then it gets out of my way so I can workout.

Edit 6/2021. I've been using this for several years now & love it. I just learned that I can add a web link to comments. My MD recommended stretching exercises for sciatica so now I can go directly to the site from FitNotes. Original comment: This app is extremely flexible & easy to use. I can create my workout before heading to the gym & change it on the fly if a particular piece of equipment is already taken.

This app is an absolute gem. I'm doing my own routine and after three workouts it knows the increments I use for squats and bench (5kg, 2.5kg) respectively, and increments them for me when I want to log a new workout. So powerful and simple to use! The developer deserves all the credit in the world for making the app so good and releasing it free and without adds. More power to him and I wish to use more apps by him in the future.

Really like this app. Edited on 9/23/2020 I've been using this app for over a year now and find its the best workout log app I could find. I tested quite a few others but they all have their flaws. I really like how you can easily access your history for each exercise so you know what weight to use and how many reps you need to do for a PR. I also like how they allow you to create full routines, unlike other apps which just allow you to create a individual days or workouts.

With this app I can record my workouts with 100% accuracy. It is feature packed and took me some time to realize everything it could do but it has made my workouts more efficient. I don't have to search through hundreds of variant exercises like on MyFitnessPal, no online connectivity needed, can create routines and set individual rest timers for different exercises. Historical information and graphs are incredibly useful.

The BEST no frills, just let me track my workout app. Doesn't waste my time with ads or awful illustrations like so many others. Has all sorts of graphs and useful calculators (1RM, plate calculator, timer) built in. You can use just about everything you need free. After using this for months I have tossed the dev $5 via the supporter app, because this is what a fitness tracker should be.

The only app that I use each time I go to the gym and cannot believe it's free with no ads. I love how it utilizes graphs to help you track progressive overload. Also shows your would-be 1RM as well to gauge your strength and set your goals. Took a few gym trips to figure out how it works but it is very simple and will help you towards reaching your fitness goals. 10 out of 5 stars 🌟.

This is the first and best workout tracker I have ever used. It lets you customize workouts, create custom lifts, use custom bars, track long term progress, and based on the bar it tells you which weights to put on the bar. Some additions I would like to see is making bar management more intuitive, so I can select a specially weighted bar for multiple lifts. I would also like to track single leg exercises by reps per leg, and enter individual dumbbells.

This app does has everything I need without the extra bulk. It excels in speed, customizability, cleanliness, and the price! (it's free, if you don't have the need to customize). The only suggestion I can say is the share function. While sharing works, and you can share the workouts you've done for the day, it would be a bit better if I can share my workouts for the week so I can share it with my personal trainer. Thank you! This is an awesome app!

I really do love this app - great for tracking weights & cardio, you can add in as many routines/exercises as you wish, track your body measurements, and you get to see your progress via graphs in the analysis feature. The only thing that I wish it had was an ability to take progress pictures! I know many would appreciate it for various fitness goals. Commenting on your workout is a nice touch if you like to add notes, the calender highlighting the muscles you worked is 🔥 I could go on!

Took a little while to get used to, and I still only utilize 25% of the apps capabilities, but it is very useful for someone who wants a place to quickly add and access workouts. I have used this app for over a year, and it has been great for me to keep track of my progress. The UI could use a tune up, but the things I was looking for are easy to access, so I have no complaints overall.

I love how simple this app is but it has everything you need, it's not an app that's over complicated. also doesn't have any ads or any subscription which is one of the main reasons why I don't like many other apps like this one. I also love how you can add a custom workout that the app might not have. definitely the best free workout tracking apps.

One of the best fitness apps out there. It makes keeping track of my routine, my sets/reps, and weight super easy. Update: I've now been using this app for years. Still haven't found one better. I even paid for the supporter app a while back. I rarely ever pay for apps of any kind, but this one deserves it.

Incredibly clean, no nonsense tracker which has helped me track my workouts and make great gains. There's no pressure to log body weight, or pop ups to wade through. You just see results update: I am now down 6% body fat and up 16lbs of muscle. The only feature I would like to see added is an option for incorporating a decreasing assist weight. I do assisted pullups and my graphs are a little funky because the weigh is going down instead of up

Hate that all my info is gone when I got a new phone. Anyway to setup an account to keep back up data? But, with that said. Best app for tracking progress and easy to use. Goal section is perfect for those who want a timed goal such as small wins until you reach your ultimate goal. Great app. Used for years

This is absolutely the best app. Logical, customizable, easy to use interface, easy to modify/add workouts. Easy to track progress, and my regime has improved in part thanks to this app. I wouldn't change a thing. And the little trophies are great incentives too! 6 out of 5 stars... and I just found out there's a paid version. I will very happily pay 6 bucks to the genius who figured this one out!

Just started the gym 2 months ago. I kept putting off getting a notebook. So many apps for anything I do are frustrating and I end up never using them and decided a notebook would be easier. I can never remember my previous weight on a machine. I saw the rating for this app and downloaded it and nearly right away I can type in exactly what I need. No learning curve. Every piece of equipment. 5 stars.

For a free app this is all you need. The lists of exercises are really good, with the ability to edit and customize to specific needs. It's easy to use and understand, mind you, what would make it better would be a user manual or beginning walkthrough of the app. It also has body analysis tracker and plate calculator which are nice to have. Another thing that would make it even better would be the ability to link it to Google fit. But! For free, as I said it's a must have. Very good 👍.

This app is amazing. Perfect for tracking sets, reps, and weights. I've been using it for two years, and it is incredible. There are PR calculators, Plate Calculators, % and Rep Calculators, and all kinds of analytic tools for showing improvement. I've never had the app crash, and it is really easy to backup. I'm in the gym 3-5 times a week, and I've had the app for 2.5 years, so that should tell you how good it is.

Ive been using this app for the past 5 years. Very simple to use! Allows me to add any exercise i want, record reps and sets, create routines, etc. Definitely would suggest for someone looking for a way to track and organize their workouts. With so many fitness trackers costing money nowadays and charging for access to the simplest and bare minimum features, its nice to have an app with no limited accessibility features.

I like most of this app except I can't for the life of me figure out how to get it to show that I've ever completed my routine. I go through the whole routine and it even acknowledges that I finished it but it still says "Last Completed Never". All it's good for is tracking individual exercises. Edit: Yes, Selasoft, I did everything exactly as you described and when I click on "finish" it still doesn't show that the routine has ever been completed.

Simple, straightforward logging that doesn't try to build your workout for you. Exactly what I was looking for. Could the interface use a bit of polish? Sure. • exercise name headers often get cut off in s routine and require jumping into the history and back to get them to be accessible • no way to end a routine at any time. If you do it out of order and complete the last element early you lose the ability to cleanly end your workout. Still give stars as it's free and nearly perfect.

I love this simple app. It records my routine and my workout. I get a summary after each workout. 3/8/2026: I've been using it since 2024 and it is my gym companion, I don't have to mentally track my previous workout. still works great, so easy, and simple to use. Developers are responsive. Is it perfect? For me, not yet, pretty darn close though. It's free, I don't notice the ads most of the time since my eyes are on the screen only briefly between sets/exercises. download it and try it

been using it for years. good app. a few things I'd like to see a change on 1) and improvement of the super set, as of now. you have to save and exit an exercise just to log your 2nd exercise of the super set. I feel when you have them logged together in a S.S. you should go back and forth without you having to save, exit, go back to history and edit every time you want to start your new set, 2) a time option. for when your doing an exercise that involves time instead of reps.

Edit 4/11/2026: This is still my go to app nearly 4 years later. Ads are non-invasive and minimal and it doesn't lock basic features behind a pay-wall. If you're handy with spreadsheets, you can export/import your data to create your own charts & visualizations. It's everything I need, no fluff, just stuff.

An annoying thing is in all the time I've used it they still haven't fixed the "last completed" bug. Instead of showing when you last completed an exercise, it appears to be reporting when you FIRST completed it, so it never changes and is useless. Otherwise, like the name says, it's simple. It's not cluttered with useless junk, but it's easy to add your own exercises, and the ads are mostly unobtrusive and don't try to take over your phone. With some exceptions like "last completed", it works.

I've been using the free version app for 2 years now. It's a great app, easy to use, with charts/graphs to view progress, you can add your own routines and exercises. Easily navigated, great colourway. I like that you can use it when your phone screen is locked for extra security/peace of mind. I would highly recommend it. its been really helpful with motivation, tracking and accountability. You can also easily share the summary of your workout to other apps/PT as a pic. Shame it's not on Apple.

I enjoy how easy it is to setup your routines with the preloaded exercises. You can also easily create your own exercises if the app doesn't have it. Overall very satisfied with the app! I've been using it for a year now and for it being free I cant complain! The only little annoyance I have is sometimes the app will just close itself out in the middle of a routine, like it crashes and when you open up the app again the routine is gone and it didn't save any of the recorded sets.

This workout tracker has been my go-to for years, and I still love it. I've used other exercise apps with bells and whistles, but SWL is the easiest to set up, use while in the gym, and go to later for looking at progress. Edit: The ads on the free version are annoying, but any app you pay for *once* to remove ads, in an amount you might spend at Starbucks, is well worth it.

It does exactly what it is supposed to do and does it very well. Ads are unobtrusive, such that I rarely notice them. There are no annoyances with this app. Signing in is optional, so no account required unless you want your data backed up to the cloud. There are no nag screens asking you to enable endless permissions. In fact, the only notification you will receive is when the timer in between sets expires. I wish more developers followed this example as it is truly refreshing.

This is simply the best workout tracker out there. Easy Easy Easy to use. Tracks history. Keep notes for each exercise inside of that particular exercise.. Glance at what you did last session inside each category. ie..inside of leg press you have a history button to track last time you did it. I looked at a lot of workout apps and none were as simple to use. Try it! FR

It's easy to create new exercises if they're not already in the list. Editing entries is also easy for the times I enter the wrong weight or too many or few sets. I really like the feature for entering free-text notes. EDIT: I've been using the app a couple years now, and I still love it. The only thing I wish the app had that it doesn't is a macro amd calorie tracker.

Very nice to use, once you get past the learning curve. The developer is very responsive. Very customizable. For example, for exercises I do at the gym on their machines, l prefixed the exercise name with initials of the gym to keep them all together. I set up a routine for each day. Exercises can be moved around within a routine. Your data can be exported by the app as a .CSV file (which can be read by any spreadsheet program) to your email.

Just switched to this from another app that was trying to shove their social media down my throat, making it harder and harder to actually, ya know, TRACK MY WORKOUTS. This one does what it says, with no extra junk. I do have one feature request though: in the weight plate calculator, can you please add a check box for 7.5lb plates? Thanks! Question: when I add a set in a superset, should it move to the next exercise in the SS? It doesn't but I expected it to...

It's simple and easy to use. It doesn't have in the exercise menu that I use, but in the comments of an exercise that is somewhat similar I just make a note of exercise II actually did. I finally used the plus sign at the bottom right which allows me to add exercises of my description. I would like to find this app to use for multiple personal training clients.

thus far it's been excellent on tracking my workouts. I'm 71, a widower, and simply trying to get back in shape. this app is instrumental in tracking what I'm doing, seeing how my progress is, and an overall indication of where I'm at. I would only have one suggestion. if you've set up a routine with exercises in it, as you complete an exercise have that exercise turn green or some other color so you know that that one has been completed.

I have been an on and off weight lifter for years, and while I cannot credit my recent longest track of working out to this app entirely, it has provided me with the exact workout tracker I needed. After one use I became familiar with the app and have used it almost daily. I appreciate the calendar tool, allowing me to look back and see how many days out of the month I worked out. It keeps me going.

No account bs, no tracking. Just a simple way to log your workouts. I wish I could change the notification volume or noise for when you're done with a set, but that's not a big enough complaint to knock a star. Update after 1 year of use: This app is still great. I love the workout summaries and weight tracker, simple as it is.

The summary lists the exercises last performed, but does not note the routine completed. With 30 routines I have a separate list of where I am. If the routine name was included in the summary, that solution would make this a 5 star app for me and eliminate the second list. I've also have categories for each machine with associated exercises, would be nice to calibrate the incremental to each category, say 2.5 or 5 lbs, the only option's are 1 or .1 pounds which defaults for all categories.

It was a little tricky to figure out at first how I wanted my exercises to be setup. Also, it took some time to enter my exercises and group them properly. However, it works so well for me now. The only complaints I have are my phone locking each time I submit my set and the app asking me if I want to save my set a second time when only viewing my history for the exercise without making any changes.

This is the most customizable app to keep track of workout names and sets etc. Each day you enter your exercises. You can name them. There are no hard templates forced onto you. Basically you create the name of exercises, chart how many reps and weight or distance and calories. It's as close to wiring a journal by hand as you can get. Look up your history of sets and reps easily in the history tab.

This is a decent app, but has one thing makes it not very usable for me. I set up my workout for the day before going to the gym. I use the new routine function to record each days workout and name it with that days date. That is the only way I've found to be able to set up my workout ahead of time. The problem is, that I can find no way to set up the reps and weight ahead of time. The app says that I have to hit the "completed set" button before I can save it. I already know those numbers, so I have to write them in a separate note app to refer to and then enter the numbers in this app as I do the exercise. I would really like to be able to have the rep/weight information pre-entered before I go to the gym.

Really a great app. Pretty simple which is a good thing. Very useful when I need to remember how much to lift. I would like to see how much weight I've lifted in total ever not just in the latest workout. Obviously not necessary but could be cool. All the people who say the app is confusing are plain wrong. It is VERY simple. If you can't understand this app there's really no hope for you. The hardest part is that to add a new excersise to your routine, you need to create it first.

Glorious and simple. Tracks workout histories and allows full manual exercise selection. Also has a little rest timer between sets. The app is basically Notepad but for gym bros. Really nice considering how everything else requires a freaking account. Huge props for the dev adding a one time purchase for like $3 to permanently to remove ads. Would like to see improved superset tracking. Right now I have to end one exercise early and edit later which disables the rest timer.

Great app. Simple in this case does not mean basic. It's very easy to use and accessible. Exercises and workouts are easy to log; Interface is clean and straightforward; Timers, summaries, tools, etc...everything in here just works. Quick and easy logging let's you focus on the workout and not waste time. There's a reason this app is so highly rated. Well done!

Great app. But a few suggestions: 1) Enable changing an exercise AFTER it's been logged (Note: not just renaming). After logging an excercise I sometimes wish to change the type of excercise to something new I've added or something to fix a mistake originally selected. Allow Edit to do this. 2) Enable muti-selecting excercises in CATEGORIES using check boxes like in Routines rather than just one at a time. Thanks!

Exactly what I've been looking for. I had been using just my notes app for simplicity. This isn't much more complicated unless you want it to be, but will let me better track my progress. only thing I wish this (and most fitness apps) had was an ability to group by This Trip versus Day, if that makes sense. I tend to go late and my workouts overlap midnight, so it separates my days funny. Been manually adjusting the times for anything at 12am to compensate, so its not the end of the world.

By far one of easiest to use workout logs. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is that it doesn't have an option to delete an entire excersise log. Only lets you delete individual workouts. I did a lot of messing around my first month or so and would like to start my log over. Guess my only option is to delete app and download again.

Been using this app for a couple years now. It's easy to use and has all the features you need to accommodate just about any routine. Timer, calendar, weight calculator all in one app. You can share your routines and logs quickly if you are into that sort of thing. Only quirk is downloading all the premade workouts but once you do that it's smooth sailing.

Pros: Simple and to the point. Auto caps makes entering new workouts on the fly super easy. No intrusive ads. Cons: I wish I could set the date to a session so that when I my workouts extend through midnight, I dont have to change the date every single time I do a different workout to keep them all on the same day instead of across two days.

Love this tool! My only complaint is when you use Routines, it forces you to execute the exercises in the order they're listed. That's rarely possible in a public gym, when you do just open the Routine and do the exercises as the equipment is available, the Routine never shows as completed. I could be using it wrong, but I don't think I am.

The name of the app describes what it does. It's super simple and straight forward. Couple of additions that would be great to see would be a burnout option and body weight options just for my own peace of mind. I like seeing exactly what I did in my workout and having to type my body weight Everytime could be replaced with a single input.

Works well for me. Only complaint is when running a routine, can't seem to handle having to do exercises out of order. It lets you skip one, but doesn't automatically circle back around to it though you can go back to an exercise manually. Normally when you complete the last exercise in a routine it asks you if you want to finish. But if I skipped a n exercise I'm not finished. If I cancel that finish prompt, I don't get another finish prompt when I complete the skipped exercise.

this app is aptly named--it is simple to use and well organized. I really like the 'category' tab-makes finding your exercise easy. I love the 'summary' tab too. My husband and I like to see who lifted the most weight during our workouts and this tab gives you that info (he always wins but it's still fun). I also like the calendar so I can see all the days I've worked on any given month. I really love this app!!

I'm so grateful for this app. It's simple, ads are non invasive, it has a cloud which helps me save my data. I tried notebook tracking and it didn't work but this did. It takes a bit of initial legwork to get the app set up for your workouts but the hour of effort is so worth it. I've made more progress tracking my exercises with this app than I have any other way.

Simple and elegant, but with a learning curve. The cloud backup process is tedious and manual. Also, there is a large amount of manual steps to add an exercise to the exercise database, then to add it to a routine. Perfect for keeping up with your progress and keeping/updating a tight routine. Worth taking the time to get started.

Great no-frills app. I keep finding new features built into this app (like the advanced metrics and graphs) but the base is a very simple workout logger. I wouldn't reccomend this for time-based or cardio workouts, but it is the best app I have ever used for tracking weightlifting. It has a built in 1RM calculator, as well as a plate tool if you're bad at doing benchside math. I have been using this app for around 6 months now and it is a critical part of my workout programming.

Exactly what I was looking for. I can track my workouts, I can create routines, there's a timer embedded for in between sets. It has a simple interface and is easy to use. The only challenge about this app is if you are unsure of the name of an exercise or machine, but a quick internet search usually solves it.

Great app! Wish it had a flexibility/ stretching option for "type." I have been using the strength as the type, but it still would be nice to not have 30 second stretch interfere with the data for reps. Same complaint for exercises that are for time. But it's really not THAT big of a deal. Overall, great app!

Really good way to keep tabs on your workouts/ progress, and to be able to build a plan that's easy to take with you and follow. Only constructive criticism I have is I wish there was a way to set an exercise as "bodyweight" for things like pull ups or push ups. But that's just a drop in the ocean, A+ app!

This is my first day using the app. The good: A simple to use app for logging different aspects of my health journey. The middling: There are adds but they are small across the bottom of the app and can be removed for a price. My thoughts for improvement: make the units of measurement changeable for different exercises instead of global. I would like to annotate my rowing in meters and my walks in miles but this doesn't appear possible at this time. Possibly add other mass measurements too.

Awesome app! I like to create my own routines and I sometimes alter exercises, this app allows me to have complete control over the workout. All it does is track it so I can see my progress! I will add: I think it could be better if I could schedule a routine on the calendar. Like add future workouts so I can see what's scheduled next

Excellent and does everything you would want for a fitness tracker app. No nonsense, you can enter your own data or download a table to use. In my opinion, add your own if you already have a routine, way easier that way. I like everything about this and frankly cannot think of any negatives.

This app is excellent, easy to use and has a lot of usefull functionalities. I have been able to increase the weights I am lifting safely by checking how I progress overtime for each exercise. One thing that might be added is a summary where u see the categories u have done overtime so u can track and keep balance. Still, it's a great app and would recommend for everyone

Update 4/16/23 I still enjoy using this app to track and measure progress. Uncomplicated and simple: just what one wants in a fitness log. I use this app after every weightlifting workout to record and track my progress. Very simple and effective. I could see it being even more useful by actually bringing it into the gym and applying features such as the timer to help maintain a desired intensity level.

I've used this app for the last 3 years to track my fat loss and muscle gain progress and in all that time its never crashed or had a bug. It's exactly as advertised, just a simple electronic log for your physical health. Nothing flashy or complicated, and I never even notice the ads. You can choose from a preset list of exercises or create custom ones and add notes to each (like if a certain exercise was too easy or too hard or what level you set the treadmill too).

Nice app and makes it really easy to track workouts, create routines or just do them on the fly at the gym. The only BIG ANNOYANCE is the completed workouts are ALWAYS listed in reverse order and the export to Excel also has everything reversed. EXTREMELY annoying and hard to follow. i like to look at data in chronological order as weel as see my exercises and their sets in order. Not the last exercise first with the first set to last, then 2nd to last exercise, etc. PLEASE UPDATE IT!!!

Update: removing a star because the notes section no longer keeps formatting. I'll make new lines to comment on each individual set and then it just smashes it into one huge paragraph 😮‍💨 The name speaks for itself. Simple, useful app that allows you to organize exercises/routines with great metrics and stats. Plus the ads aren't constant intrusive popups. Somethings are annoying like the calendar can't log a routine for an at-a-glance look and sorting the exercises post-creation is a hassle.

This app gives a lot, especially for free. First, I like the simplicity, it's stripped down and doesn't over-complicate things. Next, it let's you flexibly manage you routines. I bucket up a bunch in to different themes and can bounce around them. Across routines, exercises keep their history to see what you last did. Last, I like the summary, just wish it retained those units in missing something. Area of improvement, cardio can be done in sets and measured by more than time.

I love this app. Super simple and straightforward. I just wanted something to log my reps and sets and this came with some great additional tools like the ability to build routines, see your progress over time through graphs, and estimated 1RM based on current ability. Highly recommended if you want to build your own routines and don't need the bloat of software that comes with all sorts of videos and instruction.

I never rate apps. Never. But they killed it with this one. For me it's exactly what I need and nothing else. Simple enough that even me knowing next to nothing about exercise could easily dial in a routine and log my progress. It comes pre loaded with a ton of popular exercises. Add new workouts and build custom plans with ease. No adds, no BS.

Great. I've been using it for q few years, and I'd only change two things: recently I started using metric weights and changed the app from imperial, but now all my prior workouts say 60 kg when it should have been 60 lbs or 27 kg. I wish the old weights weren't affected, because now my progress chart looks like massive losses instead of gains. Additionally, it would be nice to rearrange the tabs at the top.

The app works great, and of the many workout apps out there, this has come closest to what I need. I would really like to be able to schedule workouts on the calendar. I have small routines saved, and it would be great to be able to add multiple routines to a calendar day to create a unique workout, instead of having to create a new routine from scratch for what I want to do on a given day.

Well-designed, simple, and effective. I tried 4 apps and stuck with this one. I know what I need out of a workout and don't want to be "sold" on anything. Simple Workout Log requires no e-mail sign-up and therefore does only what I need it to; download the exercises you want (and rename them if you wish), build custom workouts, and track progress on a variety of statistics. There are also great features included like cloud backup. If you don't want unnecessary distractions I highly recommend this app.

Simple design with minimal space taken for advertisment. Replaced the notepad and pen that I had always used to track progress. Has a list of exercises that can be loaded and deleted to my liking. Also, I can switch phones without ever losing my data, thanks to the cloud backup. I have tried multiple apps and this is the one for those who have a basic understanding of fitness and do not need instructions on how to perform exercises.

The advantage of this app is simplicity and lightness. It is easy to figure out, set up and navigate. It also allows for flexibility during the workout. I would like to see one addition though: we create routines and "think" in terms of them, but in all history tools (calendar, history) routines names are not displayed. Perhaps a list of dates and used routine names under calendar? Pleasant application! I was looking for something like this!

Seems good. One bug, every once in a while the top bar (exercise name and superset arrows) disappears and I can't get it back without exiting the routine. Also, would be nice if exercises with longer names would wrap to the next line. It can be hard to see exactly what I scheduled with only the first third of the exercise name. Edit: would be nice if there was a way to make copies of routines. I use the same warmup and cool down all the time, and would also be great for variations on routines.

Wow! Easy to use and easy to add or change exercise types, weight adjustments, etc. Also keeps track of body weight. Wish there was a tool for keeping up with body measurements (I'm starting out after 6 surgeries and a year of extremely limited mobility) as one of my calves has lost a little muscle and a lot of strength, but I can keep up with that elsewhere if I don't find a good place to do it in this app. Otherwise, this app is definitely going to be my go-to in the gym.

Overall good app. Would like to see maybe a diary spot to where if you change a routine, diet etc. you can keep track of it. example 6 week routine that you can put start and end dates on calendar to know when you have made it through or just some way to track it easier than clicking and searching. Maybe a spot for goals. If anything else comes up I will review it and give input. Thanks.

Excellent, easy to use app. I agree with all of the other 5 star posts so no need to rehash. I use the Excel export extensively but I'm a spreadsheet kind of guy anyway. One suggestion: I travel internationally and weights can be in either lb. or kg. When I change units in "settings" my entire log changes unit labels but does not change the number to be the equivalent. It would be convenient to be able to work in both units depending on the gym's equipment.

Great for tracking your exercise, easy to navigate. Only the routine section is unnecessarily complicated. I don't understand the part about connecting exercises on the left, it immediately completed after first exercise. The graph section tracks max weight, when you click on the text it actually gives a number of options. Ads are not invasive.

Very easy to use. Love that you can add your own workouts and routines. Only wish was that there is a superset mode so you don't have to complete an exercise in your routine then go back to the exercise history and edit the workout to add your second or third set. ****** UPDATE: You CAN add supersets! Thanks to the dev for responding and taking the time to explain how to do it!!

Very easy and simple app. I use it to keep track of my resistance training days. You customize your own exercises and name them whatever you want and it'll save them in the exercise list. It will also keep a record of your past workouts for up to a year so you can look back at your progress. My exercise list has grown pretty big so I do wish they had an A-Z option or search option so you can skip to that letter instead of scrolling and scrolling way down to find your exercise. Other than I love this app, it's by far the best workout log I've used.

Been using this app for 6 months now. Excellent. Purchased the Pro key. All my lifts are tracked and recorded. Five stars. My only recommendation is when running a workout (Doesn't matter if it is a routine or not) you are unable to quickly switch between sets. I know there is the option for supersets (which I use) but these must be predetermined ahead of time. That works great, but occasionally I throw another workout in on the fly between sets and would like the oppurtunity to record it real time as well.

You can track sets and weights for any exercise you choose. They can be categorized freely. It's simple and easy to use while also allowing you to customize it to your preferences. Cant imagine a better app than this without it getting too complicated. Bumping to 5 stars because the respond well to customer's feedback.

Seems like a solid workout tracker but I think it should have a description of the exercises. Maybe I'm missing something. I was looking more for something I could use with my weight machine that would also motivate me to workout. N I don't really see where to add custom workouts. Again prolly user error. N my machine doesn't really have the weights listed. I usually go by how many bars n whether it's on 1, 2, or 3 on the weight multiplier. Also would be cool if there was an estimated calories

It really is a simple workout log, which is why it is wonderful. It lets me track my progress without a bunch of unwanted clutter or options I don't want or need. It also has a timer that I use for rest time between sets which I love. My only problem is that I can only enter info one exercise at a time.

Outstanding! This is the app I wished for years existed... and it actually does exist. There's freedom to enter my own equipment and routines instead of stuck with someone else's preloaded or limited choices. The only odd flaw I see is sometimes it says the last time I completed an exercise is 'today' despite it being days ago I did that exercise. Otherwise the app is perfect!

Takes a little bit of tinkering to set up but once you get all your exercises entered it's really easy to record the weight lifted and the number of reps and sets. It also keeps good records of your past workouts. Only complaint I have is that their website is broken, I can not register at their website to set up and account to use the online cloud back up feature of the app

Simple, no frills log that will help you get and stay on track for whatever your fitness goals are. One drawback though is it could improve on tracking cardio/bodyweight exercises but other than that, if you want a simple, clean, easy to follow, chart and customize workouts, this is THE app.

The new update has made the font smaller, and I can't figure out a way to make it larger. I love this app for its simplicity, but wearing readers in the gym isn't practical. I have several clients who are 40 plus who I've recommended this app to who are now looking at other options. Myself included. Hope there is a quick fix.

Does what I need it to. Let's you add exercises yourself instead of forcing you to stick with a pre-made list. The countdown timer is excellent for keeping track of rest between sets. Seeing your history with lifts or runs is great. Definitely recommend. It really is simple and easy to use.

I paid for this app to remove ads. Now you're putting ads BACK into the app I paid for? I will no longer recommend it to people who ask me about my training regime. Telling me I need to install another app? That one is trying to recharge me $4.99 that I already paid. After troubleshooting to find why my GS7 is ad free, tech asked for receipt. Didn't keep $5 app receipt from 5 years ago.

Exactly what I wanted. Well thought out user interface, and it does everything that I need for it to do. There is a good FAQ in the Tools menu that serves as a user manual, and also a helpful video overview (which I wish I had found before taking an hour or more to figure it out myself from trial and error). I am looking forward to the IOS edition when I will buy the ad-free version.

Easy to use, sturdy, non-intrusive ads. Hope you can implement the function to auto convert between kilograms and pounds. I entered my weight in kg, but for workouts I use pounds so when I switch measurement systems it only changes the label instead of converting. Also the keyboard only allows entry of numerals.. the + - keys etc dont work for me.

Complicated at first sight, this app is flexible and straightforward after a few minutes of usage. The app is a great record keeper with some useful statistics. Seeing my past results motivates me to zoom past them. I can save custom routines and exercises. I haven't used any other apps like this and i don't even know if they exist and i don't care, since this gives me everything i need.

Wonderful app! One feature request would make it perfect: It would be really great if you could switch between lbs and kgs for weight entry, from one session to the next -- yet have the weights recorded and displayed consistently. You'd still have to pick a unit for display (i.e. the unit for displaying all history and graphs). So, if you chose kg for display, history would show actual kg for the days you entered in kg, and converted lbs->kg for days you entered in lbs. For those of us who work in multiple gyms with both kg plates and lb, this would be perfect.

Great app, the only area I don't see needed is the use of Excel. A desktop version would be a great addition. Other than that it's great. I love the tracking of how much weight I've lifted over the weeks. Along with being able to build my own routines to is a big help. There was something else but can't remember. Great app in all.

The app the Y provides is junk compared to this app. They have really thought this through. You name the exercise and can rename it. You customize your sets. it records the history. Fantastic! Even though it's free, we're going to buy the Pro version. It'll be great to support a good developer!

I love this app! I love tracking the progress and that fact that it has a calendar to show me how many days I have worked out or took the day off. I would give this app 5 stars if they can add sets to the cardio work outs and they can add sub-categories for example, category bench press extra category whether it's dumbbell, kettlebell, or barbell. Regardless, great app!

It does all the things you need to track progress, but I don't get why once I create a routine it doesn't automatically create a list or checklist of my routine. That to me makes more sense and is faster. Instead I gotta tap plus every time I finish a set. Please create a checklist of the routine.

Exactly what I was looking for! You can input your own exercises, create routines, adjust set/rep schemes. Perfect for a seasoned lifter who knows what they are doing, but also very user friendly for newer lifters. Timers for rest periods are included and they're adjustable, also. A calendar of past workouts makes it easy to look back and check for improvements or to make adjustments. It's the ONLY workout app I use now, and I've tried several.

This app is great for someone who is experienced and wants to create a customized routine or has a routine they use regularly. I love that I don't have to have a user name or password. I also like that I can create a lot of workout routines. This allows me to create a full cycle with varying percentages of one rep max for each workout. A lot a apps I've tried only allow you to create five routines and then you have to upgrade if you want more. If you are inexperienced, you can download exercises.

overall great app. I tried about 20 before deciding on this one. Needs a quick menu option instead of having to press the back arrow multiple times, but otherwise, well pleased. Easy to read history at the exercise level. Pulls in last recorded weight anount so you don't have to go into history to remember how to set the strength machine.

ok app and nearly 5 stars. one big thing i don't like is that the app doesn't automatically group the sets you completed in the summary. maybe it's just my user error but i was accidently adding 3 sets at a time when i was only trying to record one. i finally figured out how to delete my mistakes. I'd like to see this change so i can keep track of what i do right after i do it while I'm exercising but again maybe I'm just using it incorrectly

I like the app overall, but I wish the auto-population of previous sets was more full-featured, similar to the app Strong where you can more easily edit reps/weight and be are able to check off sets as you complete them. Additionally, I really wish the website integration was much better. The synchronization implementation is pretty clunky currently. Plus, according to the change log, the website has not been updated for several years. I think it's a tad embarrassing as the developer for the website to not even feature email authentication.

This is certainly what it claims; a simple workout log. Didn't take much time to figure out how to use for what I need, which is recording my sets and reps and making workout plans. I would appreciate if there was a way to set a target for my workouts. At the moment I can set the exercises I want to do within a workout, then record what I do, but no way to say, for example, "I want to do 5 sets of 10 reps of x exercise". Thanks, though, great overall!

Straight forward workout log. Suggestion: once an exercise is completed, the background color should change vs. tiny "completed" at the bottom of the exercise so it's easy to spot that you've done it. Also a circuit style/drop down set be available under routine. Overall I like that it's a simple easy to use workout log.

Pretty good overall. Some minor nuances like if you start a routine and back out and return the app treats it like a new workout. I cant seem to find a way to append the routine so all the exercises and sets show up in the same screen. Soon as you back out those results go in the history tab. Not a deal breaker but it would be nice if you're minutes apart to be asked if you want to continue or append the past routine. Also not sure if there is a paid adless version but I'd be interested.

The name says it all: it's a workout log that's very simple. You can import exercise names, or name them yourself. It let's you know when last you did any particular exercise at a glance. Really neat and intuitive. I can safely recommend for those of us who just want to track without going into to much detail

I use it mostly as a journal to keep track of my progress and where to start on my next session. Also it gives a list of good ideas for alternate exercises to keep your workout routine fresh. I'm sure it will do so much more than I'm using it for, but I keep it simple and it takes little to no time to document my progress in real time. 5 stars!

It's a pretty good app, it did take time to input all the additional exercises but then again, everybody doesn't always do the same exercise. My one thing about this app is I'll try to see when the last time I did a certain exercises, it'll say I did it 8 months ago or so, when it reality it was like 3 weeks ago. So it is kind of off-putting.

This is an app that does it's job very well. It makes it easy to track your exercise routines and track your progress. It's a great tool to motivate you through your fitness plan. You can enter your own exercises, create routines, and then work through the routines at the gym, or at home. The auto fill feature helps you keep track of what weights you were doing with what exercises. You could do the same with a spreadsheet, but this app is so much more convenient.

The only thing I san say needs to improve is the lack of means to track timed reps. This includes things like yoga, planks, and stretches. These are items that have sets, but are not particularly tied to weight. Other that that, it's a perfect logbook. Wish I could pay to remove ads though. Edit:. I can change the graph to reps, but that throws off the graph for weight related excercizes. Maybe put a per excercizes option? Appropriate graph can be set for each one.

I really like this app!! It really makes it simple to log my workouts... gives me the ability to add custom exercises and create routines... includes a timer for between sets ... just a really good all around app. The one thing I would like to see added would be a total time for the workout on the Summary screen.

It's a good app to track your workout, and remember what you did the other day or what muscle groups you need to workout more. Only downside a lot of excercises aren't in there, you can add it in but that just takes up time while I'm at the gym. But overall very helpful app.

I have been using this app for more than 5 years now, started in high school all the way through college. Love how customizable this is and that I can add notes, use the timer for rests, and keep track of all my exercises easily. Updated April 2022: still using this app and loving it. The only feature I wish it had was a UI for circuit training

The UI is quite cumbersome but not impossible to figure out, I have to rate 5 stars because it has the premium features other workout apps I've tried want you to pay a monthly subscription for - *Data analysis with multiple graphs (total weight, avg weight, 1rm, max reps, etc.) - *plate calculator with avaliable weights (Strong app, strength log, stronglift and others want 10$ a month for this) -auto rest timer -Edit lift history which you need bc is easy to add duplicate sets/ routines

I have been using the app for a number of years now and it is a great app that gives alot of information including trend graphs on any data entered. It really is simple to use. I also like the calendar where you can see at a glance the days that you have exercised. It includes both a cloud and local backup destination.

This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

I've tried a lot of different workout logging apps and this one has quickly become my favorite. Very easy to use at the gym and the api and web interfaces make it easy to plan workouts. One thing I would love is a widget that would let me check off a set and see my rest timer for my active workout. Not sure how hard that would be, but it would be convenient for me. Thanks for a great app!

I was using AI for a while for my workout tracking, but it easily gets clogged with all the data and information being input over the course of weeks and months. Hevy addresses exactly what I need with logging weight and exercises, and it has a rich database of exercises. It would be nice to have the ability to complete my sets for exercises from the lock screen (Android), without having to buy a smartwatch.

Tried to create a routine and the moment I had more than a couple exercises in it I had to start saving and updating the routine after adding an exercise. Then going back in and editing it to be able to add sets or more exercises all the buttons in the UI of the routine would not let me add more sets or exercises after adding one or the other. Glad I didn't go with Pro and waste money on it.

Hevy is by far the best workout app I've used. It's easy for beginners and experienced lifters alike. Every exercise includes helpful descriptions, making it simple to learn proper form. The app is clean, easy to use, and the premium version is very affordable. Absolutely worth downloading! Love this app!!!

Usually really great. A wide variety of exercises and good "how to" instructions. However, every time I use the app for an extended period of time (such as when I am tracking a workout) I lose the ability to click on anything within the app. I can scroll through, but cannot tap the checkmark to complete the set, or tap the three dots to reorder my workout. The only way I have been able to fix it is by closing out the app. I have had to do it multiple times during a single 1 hour workout.

I really love the primary features of this app. I've been doing a recommended full body split with a few mods to swap out exercises I don't like. it's super easy to switch things up when machines are occupied or I'm in a different gym. I also really like the calculator. Occasionally, syncing with my Pixel watch gets weird, but that might not be the app's fault. I tried one of the AI recommendations, but I didn't find much use for it. The basics are great!

I have never been so consistent and so motivated to track my workouts! I love to track my personal records and be able to push myself. 💪 So many features, even in the unpaid version. Some of the math goes a little funny when you try to share it to Instagram stories (one rep max frequently doesn't make sense), and I do wish it had more calisthenic type exercises listed. But for free, it's an incredible app.

Almost perfect! So honestly this is a great app. very easy to navigate and use. as someone who's just starting the gym, it made it a breeze to log it. I also love how when I launch a workout with the app on my phone, it syncs it with my watch for heart rate and better calorie burn calculation. my only minor issue is I wish they would update the workout and keep adding more as if im doing a specific exercise and they dont have that one, I have to go for the closest similar one to track it

Love the fact it works on my watch. Not having to bring my phone around the gym is really convenient. Sometimes it doesn't sync correctly. Not sure which product in the Google chain is responsible for adding the information to Google Health (formerly Fit) but more information would be nice. Finally, it is REALLY annoying to scroll through minutes and seconds to enter time. There needs to be a way to type time in. This was the first gym training app and I won't switch to anything else.

Great app! I highly recommend it. I became more consistent because of many reasons, like the muscle chart which shows which muscles I worked out over the week, and logging is pretty seamless. It's easy to replace an exercise in your routine while you're working out if a machine isn't available. Also, it displays your PRs and the amount of weight you lifted in a session, which is encouraging. I'm very impressed with the team. They're pretty active on Reddit too, constantly seeking feedback.

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. It can also seamlessly sync with Galaxy Watch. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

Decent app. Wish there were filters to follow people based on age, country, etc. Tough to find people at my age and fitness level otherwise. The profile comparison feature is nice, but limited to only barbbeĺl exercises which is a poor design choice. Statistics are fairly thorough but a tad clunky IMO. Restricted to four saved routines for free version (understandable) is a tad limiting. Very responsive team on the bug reports. Overall, a good fitness tracker.

I have always logged my workouts for the sake of keeping track of my progress. The problem is that I use to write everything down - the old fashioned way, pen and paper (I know, I'm old). I decided to give this app a try for the sake of efficiency and convienence. Long story short, this app did not disappoint. It has everything in it I could ask for. I don't have a lot of experience in workout apps, but I know I have no need for a different one. Great job Hevy! - A consistent user

Great tool for keeping track of your routines, easily edit and add what you want per week or if your feeling a little gassed out removing sets and exercises is really easy but keeps your routine the same for thr next time if you want. I appreciate the alerts too after rest times are up so if your caught day dreaming to a song it gets yah back on track. going to buy the lifetime membership once my 3 month tracking runs up, just so I can keep the progress logged long term.

Best free app for tracking workouts and measuring progress. Used a different app for a while until they started locking exercises unless you paid for a premium account. This app has a huge library of exercises with a bunch of different variations and anything that is missing you can add yourself as a custom exercise.

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

Great app for tracking your workouts and inexpensive compared to other apps (You pay the same amount for lifetime access as you would for a year of other apps). Granted, this app does not create workouts for you... but, in my mind, ID rather go to a free AI product and create a workout there and pay less here than pay a stupid amount of money to have the apps AI make something up. Plus, this way I have a lot more control over the final workout. Definitely worth a download to check it out.

Really great app. The free version has literally everything I need to track my workouts. I love how easy it is to use. I can swap exercises in my program with a couple taps. I love the exercise library and pre-con programs. There's also a plate math calculator!! And you can customize it!! No ads, no bloat, just a great tool. The social aspect is an added bonus! Don't sleep on this app.

I love this app for tracking my workouts! It's so easy to use and customize. I love that it syncs with Strava, too, since the cardio tracking isn't quite as good as the weightlifting tracking. It's super fun receiving medals for progress! It provides helpful info on correct form and has pretty much every exercise you could want. If you have a lot of different routines, you'd need to upgrade, but I'm perfectly fine with the 4 free ones.

Absolutely love this app. I love that it includes animations about how to do specific workouts, and I love all the growth tracking features. I feel like I've made more progress in the last two months with this app than I have in years without it! There is also a certain "game" element that I enjoy, in that I am constantly trying to beat my own records. It also contains tons of premade exercise routines. I never pay for apps, but this one was worth it.

I would like a larger variety of exercises that don't require equipment as much. I only have a bench and some adjustable weights. So I feel like my overall options are limited much like the space I have to work out. so far for having the app for a week I do find it convenient and easy to use and they don't try to price gouge you every month with barely anything to offer. 🧐👍

This app is great! it has everything you need to track a workout. logging exercises is easy, reviewing a graph of your past weight on each exercise is available to track progress. I use it for two separate routines at different gyms where the available weight machines are slightly different and I have to adjust the weight - Hevy reminds me of the previous weight I lifted on that routine which makes it easy for me. And, all that is on the free version!

I recommend to everyone I meet! This is user friendly and helps you with exercises if you don't know how to do them. I love you can add in your own exercises or equipment that is not in the app already. I barely tried the free trial and loved it, so I signed up for a year, but after that I may upgrade to lifetime! I love all the real life weight comparisons!

Honestly such a great app. I have been working out for literally years and have not gotten the progress I've had since I started using Hevy. It makes tracking so easy and the muscle distribution chart and heat map help me to see what areas I need to work on and what are getting overworked. If you're a gym goer, this is the app for you. Tell your friends and compare progress and exercises!!

I love everything about this app. It gives you data about what you do, never its opinion about whether you've gone too hard or not hard enough. It tracks your lifts over time, and celebrates your PRs. Thoughtful features include rest timers, a thorough list of lifts with written and video instructions for each one, and very minimal social feed. I show this app to all my gym buddies, because it's so useful without any unnecessary bells and whistles.

Very easy to use and has all the exercises I'll need. I use free version. That being said, the reason for 3 stars is that how the app measures some workouts should change. Lunges should include body weight in volume lifted. It would be nice if there was a record for total volume lifted for one exercise in a workout, not just for one single set. Sled push should include weight added, not just distance.

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

Absolutely love this app, it works great in helping you track your progress. The only true issue that I have with this app is that I copy my previous workouts for routine and not always do I complete each exercise due to time but keep them in for the next week.. that being said even if those exercises are not checked off per set, the app is still tracking those towards my volume lifted at the end thus not truly accurate in thw end. Would love to see this updated.

I have the payed version, I like the selection of equipment it matches my gym pretty good, the trainer work really good, rotates exercises and equipment so it doesn't get stale and also gi e you replacement options if there's something you don't like or if a piece of equipment isn't available, seeing the past logs and set ratings helps alot, only thing i don't like is it doesn't have some of the past history if I switch routines.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

Before I downloaded Hevy I found it so difficult to actually track my progress. This app organizes it so well and makes each is so simple to track your progress in individual exercises, muscle groups, etc. And building your own workouts as a beginner is also incredibly easy as it will filter exercises for your needs to help you get started. I've been using it for about 2 years now and have put on significantly more muscle since starting the app.

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

Great app that can track a full routine for free. No ads so far, and the subscription is inexpensive if you want to track more then one routine. Adding workouts to the routine is simple and they have have a good selection of routines for everything from beginner to advanced workouts. Each workout also shows a simplified gif and explanation of how to do the workout.

Honestly this was the secret sauce to seeing real progress in the gym as I use the app to record every weight and rep and am able to increase it every or every other week by 2.5-5lbs. On top of it you're also able to organize and create entire workout routines for yourself. Would definitely recommend, even better if you have other gym buddies so that you can compete a bit with each other using its social media features

it is mostly easy to use; there is a comprehensive collection of exercises and building a routine is intuitive. there is even an option to create an exercise if the app doesn't contain it. however, users can only build a 4 day routine for free, any more is behind a pay wall. greed, plain and simple. it could be the best, but the devs are more concerned with profit over quality. still, it's better than most. barely.

I used to track all my workouts using an actual physical journal. one of the influencers that I follow on Instagram recommended hevy I made the switch and I'll never go back. the free version allows you to create four routines that you can utilize and if you pay for the subscription you can use even more it's worth every penny but the free version is great for beginners or intermediate lifters.

Really good app for tracking workouts. It's easy to setup routines, find the right exercises, and track your progress, what muscles you've used, etc. Everything is easy to navigate and use. I've been using the free version but might upgrade just to support such a great app.

Love the free, no ad functionality, been using it for a week and im considering buying the premium (option for one time purchase is exceptional). App functionality as well as tracking is intuitive and simple, integration with other health tracking apps and wearable support is straightforward, overall an app built for the user, I wish more companies used this kind of business model, they'd make a lot more money

I've been using this for 4 months. I like that I can set my own routines and it updates with the weight/reps I did in my last session. It makes it easy to keep track of progress. The app also makes it really easy to replace an exercise with something similar when the machine/equipment may not be available.

Been using this app since ~2021 and it has been amazing overall. Likes: Tracks historical data, showing progress and strength growth. Allows for customized workout plans you can craft around what is available to you. Fun little ribbon-achievements every time you hit a new high weight per lift. (It's very motivating!) Lifetime pass is reasonably priced (and I highly recommend it). Dislikes: AI coaching plan that comes with premium could be more customizable. overall highly recommend this app.

This app makes it super easy to keep track of your workouts, gives you a monthly summary, and is overall simple to use, and all using the free version. They also reverted back to manual time entry, which is so much easier than using the scrolling wheel, so that's an easy 5 stars from me.

whatever happened to being able to change the rest time, completing sets, or anything while you have a routine running is broken. it's so frustrating to try to check off a set but even after 10 taps it doesn't go through. I tried turning the rest time off in case it was some sort of conflict in timers or something but this main thread be going wild here

Easy to use and great for beginners. I liked the preloaded routines to start and create my own routines in the app now that I'm more comfortable in the gym. I like the progress tracking graphs, it motivates me to see that I've gotten stronger. A lot of great features for a free app!

I was looking for a new weightlifting log app that had more than just the basics. I love the simplicity of the interface and the ability to discover new exercises. The user interface has intuitive options to quickly record your sets and add new exercises on the fly.

best workout app I have used, it has made tracking progressive overload so much simpler. however, I only have one problem. theres no way to make it to where ONLY your followers can see your uploads. Me and my girlfriend like to keep track of each others work outs but don't want the public seeing it so we can't do it.

straight up love this app. the ease of tracking workouts is amazing. I've tried quite a few and nothing comes close. I love the ability to easily see the weights Ive used just recently. I've always had issues I get to the gym and have to start essentially anew. 100% worth the price.

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

I really like this app, I started working out with a group of friends and this app lets us all participate and be present for each other in our workouts. It's great for finding exercises based on muscle group as well. I think this app will be a great help as I am starting to workout by myself, would recommend this to anyone in similar situations

Great app. The free version is good enough for most people, supporting up to 3 workout routine rotations. I decided to upgrade to the paid version to have the flexibility of having more routines. I love the tracking, the timing between sets, ability to do supersets (no breaks between 2 or more workouts) and the tracking of your best overall lifts. This app helped me gain 10 lb of muscle in 8 weeks. Current total gain is 15 lbs of muscle. Goal is 30 lb 💪
